classifications.title: Ádám's Conjecture
classifications.clean_statement: Every finite digraph that contains at least one directed cycle has an arc a such that reversing a (replacing arc (u,v) by (v,u)) strictly decreases the total number of directed cycles in the digraph. NOTE: This conjecture is FALSE; counterexamples are known (e.g. Thomassen / Grinberg constructed digraphs where every single arc reversal does not decrease — and in fact every reversal increases or keeps equal — the number of directed cycles).
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture adam : forall (D:DiGraph), (exists c, is_dicycle D c) -> exists a, num_dicycles (reverse_arc D a) < num_dicycles D.  (* known FALSE *)

classifications.title: Antidirected trees in digraphs
classifications.clean_statement: An antidirected tree is an orientation of a tree in which every vertex has either indegree 0 or outdegree 0 (i.e. the orientation alternates direction along every path). Conjecture (Addario-Berry, Havet, Linhares Sales, Reed, Thomassé): If D is a digraph with |A(D)| > (k-2)|V(D)| arcs, then D contains every antidirected tree on k vertices as a subdigraph. This is the antidirected analogue of the Burr/Erdős-Sós-type density condition for trees.
definitions_needed: antidirected tree (orientation of a tree with all indeg=0 or outdeg=0)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture antidir : forall (D:DiGraph) k, #|arcs D| > (k-2)*#|D| -> forall (T:DiGraph), antidirected_tree T -> #|T| = k -> contains_subdigraph D T.
classifications.attribution: Addario-Berry, Havet, Linhares Sales, Reed, Thomassé

classifications.title: Arc-disjoint directed cycles in regular directed graphs
classifications.clean_statement: Conjecture (Alon-McDiarmid-Molloy / Bermond et al. type): If G is a k-regular digraph (every vertex has indegree = outdegree = k) with no parallel arcs, then G contains a collection of C(k+1,2) = (k+1)k/2 pairwise arc-disjoint directed cycles.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture adc_reg : forall (G:DiGraph) k, diregular G k -> simple G -> exists (cs : seq cycle), arc_disjoint cs /\ all (is_dicycle G) cs /\ size cs >= (k+1)*k/2.

classifications.title: Arc-disjoint out-branching and in-branching
classifications.clean_statement: Conjecture (Thomassen / Bang-Jensen): There exists an integer k such that every k-arc-strong digraph D with two specified vertices u and v contains an out-branching rooted at u (a spanning arborescence with all arcs directed away from u) and an in-branching rooted at v (a spanning arborescence with all arcs directed into v) that are arc-disjoint.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture ob_ib : exists k, forall (D:DiGraph) u v, arc_strong D k -> exists Bp Bm, out_branching D u Bp /\ in_branching D v Bm /\ arc_disjoint Bp Bm.

classifications.title: Arc-disjoint strongly connected spanning subdigraphs
classifications.clean_statement: Conjecture (Bang-Jensen-Yeo): There exists an integer k such that every k-arc-strong (k-arc-connected) digraph D contains a pair of arc-disjoint strongly connected spanning subdigraphs (each spanning subdigraph uses all vertices of D and is itself strongly connected, and the two subdigraphs share no arc). This is the SAD (Strongly-Arc-Disjoint) spanning subdigraph problem.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture sad : exists k, forall (D:DiGraph), arc_strong D k -> exists S1 S2, spanning_strong D S1 /\ spanning_strong D S2 /\ arc_disjoint S1 S2.

classifications.title: Caccetta-Häggkvist Conjecture
classifications.clean_statement: Conjecture (Caccetta-Häggkvist 1978): Every simple digraph on n vertices with minimum outdegree at least r contains a directed cycle of length at most ceil(n/r). Equivalently, every digraph with minimum outdegree >= n/g has a directed cycle of length <= g (girth at most g). The case r=n/3 (every digraph with min outdegree >= n/3 has a directed triangle) is the most famous open special case.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture cacc_hagg : forall (D:DiGraph) r, simple D -> min_outdeg D >= r -> exists c, is_dicycle D c /\ length c <= (#|D| %/ r) + (#|D| %% r != 0).

classifications.title: Circular flow number of regular class 1 graphs
classifications.clean_statement: A nowhere-zero r-flow on a graph G is an orientation D of G plus phi:E->R with 1<=|phi(e)|<=r-1 for all edges and conservation sum_{e in E^+(v)} phi(e) = sum_{e in E^-(v)} phi(e) at every vertex. The circular flow number F_c(G) = inf{r : G has a nowhere-zero r-flow}. A graph with max degree k is class 1 if its edge chromatic index equals k. Conjecture (Steffen, truncated in source): For an integer t>=1, every (2t+1)-regular class-1 graph G satisfies F_c(G) <= 2 + 1/t. NOTE: this concerns nowhere-zero flows on UNDIRECTED graphs via an orientation; it is a coloring/flow problem, not a pure digraph conjecture, and is borderline out-of-scope for a directed-graph library; involves real-valued flows.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture cfn_class1 : forall G t, t>=1 -> regular G (2*t+1) -> class1 G -> Fc G <= 2 + 1/t. (* reals *)

classifications.title: Circular flow numbers of $r$-graphs
classifications.clean_statement: A nowhere-zero r-flow on G is an orientation D plus phi:E->R with 1<=|phi(e)|<=r-1 and flow conservation at each vertex; F_c(G)=inf{r : G has a nowhere-zero r-flow}. A (2t+1)-regular graph G is a (2t+1)-graph if |partial_G(X)| >= 2t+1 for every odd-size vertex subset X (every odd edge-cut has at least 2t+1 edges). Conjecture (Steffen): For every integer t>1, if G is a (2t+1)-graph then F_c(G) <= 2 + 2/t. NOTE: same flow/coloring family as the previous item; concerns undirected graphs via orientations and real flows, borderline out-of-scope for a pure digraph library.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture cfn_rgraph : forall G t, t>1 -> is_2t1_graph G t -> Fc G <= 2 + 2/t. (* reals *)

classifications.title: Cyclic spanning subdigraph with small cyclomatic number
classifications.clean_statement: Conjecture: Let D be a digraph all of whose strong components are nontrivial (each strong component has at least one arc / contains a cycle). Then D contains a cyclic spanning subdigraph (a spanning subdigraph in which every vertex lies on a directed cycle, i.e. has positive in- and out-degree) whose cyclomatic number (|A| - |V| + (number of connected components), the dimension of the cycle space) is at most alpha(D), the independence number / stability number of (the underlying graph of) D. Related to the Gallai-Milgram / Bessy-Thomassé circle of ideas linking cycle covers and independence number.
definitions_needed: cyclic spanning subdigraph (every vertex on a cycle)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture cyc_span : forall (D:DiGraph), all_strong_comps_nontrivial D -> exists S, spanning_cyclic D S /\ cyclomatic S <= alpha D.

classifications.title: Decomposing an even tournament in directed paths
classifications.clean_statement: Conjecture (Alspach-Mason-Pullman 1976): Every tournament T on an even number of vertices can be arc-decomposed into exactly sum_{v in V} max(0, d^+(v) - d^-(v)) directed paths, where d^+(v) and d^-(v) are the out- and in-degrees of v. (The sum is the natural lower bound on the number of paths in any path decomposition; the conjecture asserts it is achieved for even tournaments.)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture even_tour_paths : forall (T:Tournament), ~~ odd #|T| -> exists (ps : seq path), arc_partition_into_dipaths T ps /\ size ps = \sum_v maxn 0 (outdeg T v - indeg T v).

classifications.title: Decomposing k-arc-strong tournament into k spanning strong digraphs
classifications.clean_statement: A digraph is k-arc-strong if it remains strongly connected after the deletion of any k-1 arcs (equivalently, for every ordered pair (u,v) there are k arc-disjoint u-v dipaths). A spanning strong subdigraph is a strongly connected subdigraph on the full vertex set. Conjecture: every k-arc-strong tournament T has k arc-disjoint spanning strong subdigraphs D_1,...,D_k (i.e. the arc set of T can be partitioned/covered so that each D_i is spanning and strongly connected). This is a tournament special case related to Bang-Jensen and Yeo's work on arc-disjoint strong spanning subdigraphs.
classifications.duplicate_of: related to Bang-Jensen-Yeo SAD (arc_disjoint folder); tournament-restricted variant
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture: forall (T : Tournament) k, arc_strong T k -> exists Ds : k.-tuple (subdigraph T), arc_disjoint Ds /\ all (fun D => spanning D && strongb D) Ds.

classifications.title: Directed path of length twice the minimum outdegree
classifications.clean_statement: An oriented graph is a digraph with no digon (at most one arc between any pair of vertices). Conjecture (Thomasse / 'long directed path'): every oriented graph with minimum outdegree at least k contains a directed path with 2k arcs (length 2k). Note the OPG title says 'cycle' but the stated body says 'path'; the standard open conjecture is about a directed PATH of length 2k. (The cycle analogue, a directed cycle of length >= 2k+1, is a separate stronger statement.)
classifications.attribution: Thomassé (also attributed in the Caccetta-Häggkvist circle)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture: forall (D : Oriented) k, (forall v, outdeg D v >= k) -> exists p : dipath D, size p = 2*k.

classifications.title: Edge-disjoint Hamilton cycles in highly strongly connected tournaments
classifications.clean_statement: Conjecture: for every integer k >= 2 there exists an integer f(k) such that every strongly f(k)-connected tournament contains k arc-disjoint Hamilton (directed) cycles. (A Hamilton dicycle is a directed cycle through every vertex; strongly t-connected means the underlying digraph stays strong after removing fewer than t vertices.) Note: known results (Kuhn, Lapinskas, Osthus, Patel) resolve large cases asymptotically; the general bounded f(k) form is the conjecture.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture: forall k, k>=2 -> exists f, forall (T:Tournament), strong_conn T f -> exists Cs : k.-tuple (dicycle T), arc_disjoint Cs /\ all hamiltonian Cs.

classifications.title: Erdős-Pósa property for long directed cycles
classifications.clean_statement: Conjecture: fix an integer ell >= 2. For every integer n >= 0 there is an integer t_n = t_n(ell) such that for every digraph D, either D contains n pairwise vertex-disjoint directed cycles each of length at least ell, or there is a set T of at most t_n vertices with D - T having no directed cycle of length at least ell. (Directed Erdős-Pósa property restricted to long cycles.) The case ell arbitrary was a known open problem; for general directed cycles the Erdős-Pósa property holds (Reed-Robertson-Seymour-Thomas), and the long-cycle directed version is the question here.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture: forall ell, ell>=2 -> forall n, exists t, forall (D:DiGraph), (exists pk : n.-tuple (dicycle D), vtx_disjoint pk /\ all (fun C => length C >= ell) pk) \/ (exists T, #|T|<=t /\ no_long_dicycle (del_vtxs D T) ell).

classifications.title: Hamilton cycle in small d-diregular graphs
classifications.clean_statement: A digraph is d-diregular if every vertex has indegree at least d and outdegree at least d. Conjecture (Jackson): for d > 2, every d-diregular oriented graph (digon-free) on at most 4d+1 vertices has a Hamilton directed cycle. (Equivalently the bound 4d+1 is the threshold below which diregularity forces Hamiltonicity in oriented graphs.)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture: forall (D:Oriented) d, d>2 -> diregular D d -> #|V D| <= 4*d+1 -> exists C : dicycle D, hamiltonian C.

classifications.title: Highly arc transitive two ended digraphs
classifications.clean_statement: A digraph is highly arc transitive if its automorphism group acts transitively on the set of directed s-arcs (directed walks of length s) for every s >= 0. The number of ends of (the underlying graph of) the digraph is a notion from infinite-graph theory. The 'tile' of a two-ended highly arc-transitive digraph is the structure between consecutive 'levels' of its layered/reachability decomposition. Conjecture (Cameron-Praeger-Wormald): if G is a highly arc-transitive digraph with exactly two ends, then every tile of G is a disjoint union of complete bipartite graphs. NOTE: this is an infinite-digraph statement requiring ends/automorphism machinery far outside the current finite library.
classifications.rocq_sketch: (* infinite-digraph; out of finite-library scope *) Conjecture: forall G, hat_digraph G -> two_ended G -> forall tile, is_disjoint_union_of_complete_bipartite (tile_of G tile).

classifications.title: Hoàng-Reed Conjecture
classifications.clean_statement: Conjecture (Hoàng-Reed): every digraph D in which every vertex has outdegree at least k contains k directed cycles C_1,...,C_k such that for each j with 2 <= j <= k, the cycle C_j meets the union of the previous cycles (C_1 ∪ ... ∪ C_{j-1}) in at most one vertex. (A 'laminar-like' family of k cycles forming a forest of intersections.) Known to hold for k <= 3 and for tournaments/Eulerian special cases; general case open.
definitions_needed: cycle C_j meets union of predecessors in at most one vertex (laminar intersection condition)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture: forall (D:DiGraph) k, (forall v, outdeg D v >= k) -> exists Cs : k.-tuple (dicycle D), forall j, 2<=j<=k -> #|verts (tnth Cs j) :&: \bigcup_{i<j} verts (tnth Cs i)| <= 1.

classifications.title: Jaeger's modular orientation conjecture
classifications.clean_statement: Conjecture (Jaeger): every 4k-edge-connected (undirected) graph has an orientation such that for every vertex v, indegree(v) - outdegree(v) ≡ 0 (mod 2k+1). (For k=1 this is the case of 4-edge-connected graphs having a mod-3 orientation, related to nowhere-zero flows / the (2k+1)-flow conjecture circle.) NOTE: this is fundamentally about orienting an UNDIRECTED graph; it lives at the boundary of digraph theory and uses modular flow machinery. The k=1 case (and the weaker '6k-edge-connected' version) have partial results; Lovász-Thomassen-Wu-Zhang proved the 6k-edge-connected version, but the 4k bound is open and a counterexample to a strong form exists.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture: forall (G:ugraph) k, edge_conn G (4*k) -> exists (o : orientation G), forall v, (indeg o v - outdeg o v) %% (2*k+1) == 0.

classifications.title: Large acyclic induced subdigraph in a planar oriented graph
classifications.clean_statement: Conjecture: every planar oriented graph D (digon-free digraph with a planar underlying graph) has an induced acyclic subdigraph on at least (3/5)|V(D)| vertices. (Equivalently, one can delete at most (2/5)|V(D)| vertices to destroy all directed cycles; the 3/5 fraction is conjectured tight by directed/oriented planar constructions.)
classifications.attribution: Albertson-Berman style / Harutyunyan-Mohar circle (planar oriented MAS)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture: forall (D:Oriented), planar D -> exists S, induced_acyclic D S /\ 5 * #|S| >= 3 * #|V D|.

classifications.title: Linial-Berge path partition duality
classifications.clean_statement: For a path partition P of a digraph D (a partition of V(D) into vertex-disjoint directed paths), and an integer k, the k-norm of P is sum over paths p in P of min(|p|, k). Conjecture (Berge's strengthening of the Gallai-Milgram / Linial direction): the minimum k-norm over all path partitions of D is at most the maximum number of vertices in an induced subgraph of D that is k-colorable (i.e. partitionable into k independent sets / k stable sets in the sense of antichains under reachability — the max size of a union of k 'paths-meeting' ... precisely: max size of an induced subdigraph coverable by k disjoint stable/path-coloring classes). This is the Berge path-partition conjecture, the dual companion to Greene-Kleitman; equivalent to Linial's conjecture min k-norm <= max k-optimal. Known for k=1 (Gallai-Milgram) and various classes (acyclic, etc.); general k open.
definitions_needed: k-norm of a path partition (sum of min(|path|,k))
k-colorable induced subgraph / max k stable sets
minimum over partitions and maximum over induced subgraphs (min-max duality)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture: forall (D:DiGraph) k, min_over_path_partitions (k_norm k) D <= max_over_induced (fun S => k_colorableb S && #|S|) D.

classifications.title: Long directed cycles in diregular digraphs
classifications.clean_statement: Every strongly connected oriented graph (digraph with no 2-cycle/digon) in which every vertex has in-degree >= d and out-degree >= d contains a directed cycle of length at least 2d+1.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Theorem: forall (D:Oriented), strong D -> (forall v, d <= indeg v /\ d <= outdeg v) -> exists c, dicycle D c /\ 2*d+1 <= length c.

classifications.title: Minimum number of arc-disjoint transitive subtournaments of order 3 in a tournament
classifications.clean_statement: Every tournament T on n vertices contains at least ceil(n(n-1)/6 - n/3) arc-disjoint transitive subtournaments of order 3 (i.e. transitive triangles whose arc sets are pairwise disjoint).
classifications.rocq_sketch: Theorem: forall (T:Tournament) (n := #|T|), exists (F : seq (sub_tournament 3)), arc_disjoint F /\ ceil (n*(n-1)/6 - n/3) <= size F /\ all transitive3 F.

classifications.title: Monochromatic reachability or rainbow triangles
classifications.clean_statement: Let T be a tournament whose arcs are colored with 3 colors. Then T has either a rainbow directed triangle (a directed 3-cycle whose three arcs all have distinct colors) or a vertex v from which every other vertex is reachable by a monochromatic directed path (a directed path all of whose arcs share one color).
classifications.attribution: Sands, Sauer, Woodrow (variant); posed by Bang-Jensen / Aharoni et al.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Theorem: forall (T:Tournament) (c : arc T -> 'I_3), (exists tri, rainbow_tri c tri) \/ (exists v, forall u, mono_reach c v u).

classifications.title: Monochromatic reachability in arc-colored digraphs
classifications.clean_statement: For every k there exists an integer f(k) such that for every digraph D whose arcs are colored with k colors, there is a set S subset of V(D) that is the union of at most f(k) independent (stable) sets, such that every vertex of D has a monochromatic directed path to some vertex of S.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Theorem: forall k, exists f, forall (D:DiGraph) (c:arc D->'I_k), exists (S:{set D}) (parts:seq {set D}), (size parts <= f) /\ all stable parts /\ S = cover parts /\ forall v, exists s, s \in S /\ mono_path c v s.

classifications.title: Non-edges vs. feedback edge sets in digraphs
classifications.clean_statement: Let G be a simple digraph. Let gamma(G) be the number of unordered pairs of non-adjacent vertices (number of non-edges), and beta(G) the minimum size of a feedback arc set (smallest arc set whose removal makes G acyclic). If G has no directed cycle of length <= 3, then beta(G) <= gamma(G)/2.
definitions_needed: no directed cycle of length <= 3 (digon-free + triangle-free)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Theorem: forall (G:DiGraph), (forall c, dicycle G c -> 3 < length c) -> 2 * beta G <= gamma G.

classifications.title: Oriented chromatic number of planar graphs
classifications.clean_statement: An oriented coloring of an oriented graph D is a map c : V(D) -> C such that (i) c is a proper coloring of the underlying graph and (ii) for no pair of arcs (u,v),(x,y) do we have c(u)=c(y) and c(v)=c(x); equivalently a homomorphism from D to a tournament on |C| vertices. The oriented chromatic number is the least |C|. Determine the maximum oriented chromatic number over all orientations of planar graphs (known to lie between 17 and 80).
classifications.rocq_sketch: Definition ochi (D:Oriented) := \min_{k} (exists T:Tournament, #|T|=k & dhom D T). Goal: characterize \max_{D planar oriented} ochi D.

classifications.title: Oriented trees in n-chromatic digraphs
classifications.clean_statement: Every digraph with chromatic number (of the underlying undirected graph) at least 2k-2 contains every oriented tree on k vertices as a subdigraph. (Burr's conjecture; the analogous Sumner-type bound.)
definitions_needed: containment of an oriented tree as subdigraph
classifications.rocq_sketch: Theorem: forall (D:DiGraph) k (Tr:OrientedTree k), 2*k-2 <= chi (underlying D) -> contains_subdigraph D Tr.

classifications.title: The Two Color Conjecture
classifications.clean_statement: If G is an orientation of a simple planar graph, then V(G) can be partitioned into two sets X1, X2 such that each induced subdigraph G[X_i] is acyclic. Equivalently, every planar digraph has dichromatic number at most 2.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Theorem: forall (G:DiGraph), planar G -> exists (X1 X2 : {set G}), partition [set X1; X2] setT /\ acyclic (induced G X1) /\ acyclic (induced G X2).

classifications.title: Partitionning a tournament into k-strongly connected subtournaments
classifications.clean_statement: Given positive integers k_1,...,k_p, does there exist an integer g(k_1,...,k_p) such that every g(k_1,...,k_p)-strongly-connected tournament T admits a partition (V_1,...,V_p) of its vertex set where each induced subtournament T[V_i] is non-trivial and k_i-strongly connected?
classifications.rocq_sketch: Theorem: forall (ks:seq nat), exists g, forall (T:Tournament), strongk T g -> exists (parts:seq {set T}), partition parts setT /\ all2 (fun V k => strongk (sub_tournament V) k /\ 1 < #|V|) parts ks.

classifications.title: PTAS for feedback arc set in tournaments
classifications.clean_statement: Is there a polynomial-time approximation scheme (PTAS) for the minimum feedback arc set problem restricted to tournaments? I.e. for every eps>0, a poly-time algorithm returning an ordering whose number of back-arcs is at most (1+eps) times the optimum.
definitions_needed: feedback arc set in a tournament
classifications.rocq_sketch: Statement (resolved): exists PTAS for min-FAS on Tournament -- i.e. forall eps, exists poly alg, fas(alg T) <= (1+eps)*optFAS T. (Kenyon-Mathieu-Schudy: YES.)

classifications.title: Seymour's Second Neighbourhood Conjecture
classifications.clean_statement: Every oriented graph (a digraph with no digons / 2-cycles) has a vertex v whose first out-neighbourhood is no larger than its second out-neighbourhood: |N^+(v)| <= |N^{++}(v)|, where N^+(v) = {w : v->w} and N^{++}(v) = {w : w reachable from v by a directed path of length exactly 2 and w not in N^+(v) and w != v}. Equivalently there is a vertex v with outdeg(v) <= |{w : dist(v,w)=2}|.
definitions_needed: second out-neighbourhood N^{++} (out-neighbours of out-neighbours not already in N^+ and != v)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Lemma seymour_snc (D:Oriented) : exists v, #|N_out v| <= #|second_out v|.

classifications.title: Splitting a digraph with minimum outdegree constraints
classifications.clean_statement: Is there a least integer f(d) such that the vertex set of every digraph with minimum out-degree at least f(d) can be partitioned into two classes V1, V2 such that the subdigraph induced by each class has minimum out-degree at least d? (Stated by Alon; analogue of Stiebitz's theorem for undirected minimum degree. Open even for d=1, i.e. whether some constant suffices to split into two subdigraphs each of min out-degree >= 1.)
classifications.rocq_sketch: exists f:nat->nat, forall d (D:DiGraph), min_outdeg D >= f d -> exists (P:V->bool), min_outdeg (induced D P) >= d /\ min_outdeg (induced D (~~P)) >= d.

classifications.title: Stable set meeting all longest directed paths
classifications.clean_statement: Every digraph D has a stable set (independent set, no two vertices joined by an arc in either direction) S that intersects every longest directed path of D (i.e. every directed path attaining the maximum number of vertices). Generalizes the Gallai-Roy / Laborde-Payan-Xuong line; the analogue for single longest path is the Gallai conjecture context.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Lemma lpx (D:DiGraph) : exists S, stable D S /\ forall p, longest_dipath D p -> exists v, v \in S /\ v \in p.

classifications.title: Subdivision of a transitive tournament in digraphs with large outdegree
classifications.clean_statement: For every k there is an integer f(k) such that every digraph of minimum out-degree at least f(k) contains a subdivision of the transitive tournament TT_k of order k (i.e. a TT_k whose arcs are replaced by internally-disjoint directed paths). A weakening of Mader-type problems; related to Mader's conjecture that large min out-degree forces a subdivision of a large complete digraph.
classifications.rocq_sketch: exists f:nat->nat, forall k (D:DiGraph), min_outdeg D >= f k -> contains_subdivision D (TT k).

classifications.title: The Bermond-Thomassen Conjecture
classifications.clean_statement: For every positive integer k, every digraph with minimum out-degree at least 2k-1 contains k vertex-disjoint directed cycles. (Known: k=1 trivial; k=2 Thomassen; k=3 Lichiardopol-Por-Sereni; true for tournaments and for regular digraphs; general case open.)
classifications.rocq_sketch: forall k (D:DiGraph), min_outdeg D >= 2*k-1 -> exists cs, vertex_disjoint cs /\ size cs = k /\ all (dicycle D) cs.

classifications.title: Universal highly arc transitive digraphs
classifications.clean_statement: An alternating walk is a walk v0,e1,v1,...,vm where each internal vertex vi is either the head of both ei and e_{i+1} or the tail of both ei and e_{i+1}. A digraph is universal if for every pair of arcs e,f there is an alternating walk containing both. Question: does there exist a locally finite (every vertex has finite in- and out-degree) highly arc-transitive (the automorphism group acts transitively on directed walks of every length s) digraph that is universal?
classifications.rocq_sketch: Question (no clean finite form): exists D, locally_finite D /\ highly_arc_transitive D /\ universal D.

classifications.title: Woodall's Conjecture
classifications.clean_statement: If G is a directed graph whose smallest directed cut (dicut) has size k (minimum, over all nonempty proper vertex sets X with no arc leaving X, of the number of arcs entering X), then G has k arc-disjoint dijoins, where a dijoin is an arc set meeting every directed cut. (Dual / directed analogue of the Lucchesi-Younger theorem; known for k<=3 and source-sink cases.)
classifications.rocq_sketch: forall (G:DiGraph) k, min_dicut_size G = k -> exists Js, size Js = k /\ arc_disjoint Js /\ all (dijoin G) Js.

classifications.title: Conjecture 2 (1605.07411): Forb(H) chi-bounded iff H is a forest
classifications.clean_statement: For an oriented graph H, let Forb(H) be the class of oriented graphs containing no induced subgraph isomorphic to H. Then Forb(H) is chi-bounded (there is a function f such that every G in Forb(H) has chromatic number chi(G) <= f(omega(G))) if and only if the underlying undirected graph of H is a forest. Here chi/omega are the ordinary (undirected) chromatic and clique numbers of the underlying graph.
definitions_needed: Forb(H) induced-containment class for oriented graphs
undirected chi and omega via interop
classifications.rocq_sketch: Definition chibounded (F : oriented -> Prop) := exists f, forall G, F G -> chi (underlying G) <= f (omega (underlying G)). Conjecture: chibounded (Forb_ind H) <-> is_forest (underlying H).

classifications.title: Conjecture 4 (1605.07411): Forb(S) chi-bounded for every oriented star S
classifications.clean_statement: For every oriented star S (an orientation of the star K_{1,t}), the class Forb(S) of oriented graphs with no induced copy of S is chi-bounded: there is a binding function f_S with chi(G) <= f_S(omega(G)) for all G omitting S as an induced subgraph (chi, omega of the underlying undirected graph).
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture: forall S, oriented_star S -> chibounded (Forb_ind S).

classifications.title: Conjecture 5 (1605.07411): Forb of subsets of Or(P4) chi-bounded except two exceptions
classifications.clean_statement: Let Or(P_4) be the set of orientations of the path on 4 vertices. Let P be a non-empty subset of Or(P_4). If P is neither the singleton {P^+(3)} (the directed path of length 3, all arcs same direction) nor {P^+(1,1,1)} (an alternating orientation of P_4), then Forb(P) — oriented graphs with no induced copy of any member of P — is chi-bounded.
definitions_needed: Or(P4) set of orientations of P4
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture: forall (P : {set oriented}), P subset Or_P4 -> P <> Empty -> P <> {P3plus} -> P <> {P111} -> chibounded (Forb_ind_family P).

classifications.title: Conjecture 2 (1608.03040): Every digraph has a majority 3-colouring
classifications.clean_statement: Every digraph admits a majority 3-colouring: a colouring of the vertices with 3 colours such that for every vertex v, at most half of the out-neighbours of v share v's colour (i.e. the number of out-neighbours coloured the same as v is at most deg^+(v)/2).
classifications.attribution: Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen, Wood (paper authors)
definitions_needed: majority colouring (each vertex: <= half of out-neighbours same colour)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Definition majority_col (D) (c : V -> 'I_3) := forall v, 2 * #|[set u in N_out v | c u == c v]| <= outdeg v. Conjecture: forall D, exists c, majority_col D c.

classifications.title: Conjecture 9 (1608.03040): (k+1)-colouring with at most (1/k)deg^+ same-coloured out-neighbours
classifications.clean_statement: For every k >= 2, every digraph D has a vertex colouring with k+1 colours such that for each vertex v, at most (1/k)*deg^+(v) of the out-neighbours of v receive the same colour as v.
classifications.attribution: Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen, Wood (paper authors)
definitions_needed: k-majority colouring with k+1 colours and (1/k)deg^+ bound
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture: forall k, 2 <= k -> forall D, exists (c : V -> 'I_(k+1)), forall v, k * #|[set u in N_out v | c u == c v]| <= outdeg v.

classifications.title: Open Problem 1 (1608.03040): constant beta<1 for 3-colouring with beta*deg^+ bound
classifications.clean_statement: Is there a constant beta < 1 such that every digraph admits a 3-colouring in which, for every vertex v, at most beta*deg^+(v) of the out-neighbours of v receive the same colour as v?
classifications.attribution: Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen, Wood (paper authors)
definitions_needed: fractional out-neighbour bound with real constant beta
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture: exists (beta : R), beta < 1 /\ forall D, exists (c : V -> 'I_3), forall v, #|same v|%:R <= beta * (outdeg v)%:R.

classifications.title: Open Problem 2 (1608.03040): Does every tournament have a majority 3-colouring?
classifications.clean_statement: Does every tournament admit a majority 3-colouring (a 3-colouring such that for each vertex v at most half of its out-neighbours share its colour)?
classifications.attribution: Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen, Wood (paper authors)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture: forall (T : Tournament), exists c : V -> 'I_3, majority_col T c.

classifications.title: Open Problem 3 (1608.03040): Does every Eulerian digraph have a majority 3-colouring?
classifications.clean_statement: Does every Eulerian digraph (a digraph in which deg^+(v) = deg^-(v) for every vertex v) admit a majority 3-colouring?
classifications.attribution: Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen, Wood (paper authors)
definitions_needed: Eulerian digraph (deg^+ = deg^- at every vertex)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture: forall D, (forall v, outdeg v = indeg v) -> exists c : V -> 'I_3, majority_col D c.

classifications.title: Open Problem 7 (1608.03040): Is every digraph majority c-choosable for some constant c?
classifications.clean_statement: A digraph G is majority c-choosable if for every list assignment L: V(G) -> finite sets of colours with |L(v)| >= c for each v, there exists a majority colouring (each vertex has at most half its out-neighbours in its own colour) with each vertex v coloured from its list L(v). Question: is there a constant c such that every digraph is majority c-choosable?
classifications.attribution: Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen, Wood (paper authors)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture: exists c, forall D (L : V -> {set Color}), (forall v, c <= #|L v|) -> exists col, (forall v, col v \in L v) /\ majority_col D col.

classifications.title: Open Problem 8 (1608.03040): minimum total weight of a fractional majority colouring
classifications.clean_statement: Let S(G) be the set of all stable (independent) sets of a digraph G and S(G,v) those containing v. A fractional majority colouring assigns each T in S(G) a weight x_T >= 0 with sum over T in S(G,v) of x_T >= 1 for each v. What is the minimum k such that every digraph G admits a fractional majority colouring with total weight sum_{T in S(G)} x_T <= k? (It is conjectured perhaps k < 3.) Here 'stable set' is meant in the majority sense (each vertex has at most half its out-neighbours inside the set).
classifications.attribution: Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen, Wood (paper authors)
definitions_needed: fractional cover with real weights x_T
classifications.rocq_sketch: Definition fmc_value (G) := inf over feasible x of sum_T x_T. Question: sup_G fmc_value G = ? (conjectured < 3).

classifications.title: Conjecture 3 (1610.00876): TT_k subdivision forced by large minimum semi-degree
classifications.clean_statement: For every k there exists a least integer mader_{delta^0}(TT_k) such that every digraph D with minimum semi-degree delta^0(D) = min over v of min(deg^+(v), deg^-(v)) at least mader_{delta^0}(TT_k) contains a subdivision of the transitive tournament TT_k (the acyclic tournament on k vertices), i.e. a subdivision finiteness: large minimum semi-degree forces a TT_k-subdivision.
classifications.attribution: Aboulker, Cohen, Havet, Lochet, Moura, Thomasse (paper authors)
definitions_needed: subdivision of a digraph (arcs replaced by internally-disjoint directed paths)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture: forall k, exists m, forall D, mindeg0 D >= m -> contains_subdivision D (TT k).

classifications.title: Conjecture 4 (1610.00876): every oriented tree is delta^+-maderian
classifications.clean_statement: Every oriented tree F is delta^+-maderian: there exists a least integer mader_{delta^+}(F) such that every digraph D with minimum out-degree delta^+(D) >= mader_{delta^+}(F) contains F as a subdigraph (equivalently the out-degree Mader threshold is finite for every orientation of a tree).
classifications.attribution: Aboulker, Cohen, Havet, Lochet, Moura, Thomasse (paper authors)
definitions_needed: oriented tree (orientation of an undirected tree)
delta^+-maderian (finite out-degree threshold forcing F as subdigraph)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture: forall F, oriented_tree F -> exists m, forall D, minoutdeg D >= m -> contains_subdigraph D F.

classifications.title: Conjecture 7 (1610.00876): disjoint union of delta^+-maderian forests is delta^+-maderian
classifications.clean_statement: If F_1 and F_2 are delta^+-maderian (each has a finite minimum-out-degree threshold forcing its containment as a subdigraph), then their disjoint union F_1 ∪ F_2 is also delta^+-maderian.
classifications.attribution: Aboulker, Cohen, Havet, Lochet, Moura, Thomasse (paper authors)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture: forall F1 F2, maderian_outdeg F1 -> maderian_outdeg F2 -> maderian_outdeg (disjoint_union F1 F2).

classifications.title: Conjecture 11 (1610.00876): mader_chi(T) <= 2k-2 for an oriented tree of order k
classifications.clean_statement: If T is an oriented tree of order k (k vertices), then its dichromatic Mader number satisfies mader_{vec-chi}(T) <= 2k-2; i.e. every digraph D with dichromatic number vec-chi(D) >= 2k-2 contains T as a subdigraph (vec-chi(D) = minimum number of colour classes each inducing an acyclic subdigraph).
classifications.attribution: Aboulker, Cohen, Havet, Lochet, Moura, Thomasse (paper authors)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture: forall T, oriented_tree T -> #|T| = k -> forall D, dichromatic D >= 2*k-2 -> contains_subdigraph D T.

classifications.title: Problem 12 (1610.00876): value of mader_{vec-chi}(complete digraph K_n)
classifications.clean_statement: Determine mader_{vec-chi}(bidirected complete digraph K_n): the least integer m such that every digraph D with dichromatic number vec-chi(D) >= m contains a subdivision of the complete digraph on n vertices (every pair joined by a digon). The exact value/growth rate is asked for.
classifications.attribution: Aboulker, Cohen, Havet, Lochet, Moura, Thomasse (paper authors)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Definition mader_vchi_Kn n := least m, forall D, dichromatic D >= m -> contains_subdivision D (vecK n). Question: value of (mader_vchi_Kn n).

classifications.title: Problem 16 (1610.00876): are all digraphs kappa-maderian / kappa'-maderian?
classifications.clean_statement: Is every digraph F kappa-maderian (there is a finite threshold m such that every digraph D with vertex-strong-connectivity kappa(D) >= m contains F as a subdigraph)? Is every digraph kappa'-maderian (same with arc-strong-connectivity lambda = kappa' in place of kappa)?
classifications.attribution: Aboulker, Cohen, Havet, Lochet, Moura, Thomasse (paper authors)
definitions_needed: kappa-maderian / kappa'-maderian (finite connectivity threshold forcing subdigraph)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture: forall F, exists m, forall D, kappa D >= m -> contains_subdigraph D F. (and the lambda variant)

classifications.title: Antisymmetric variant of Jaeger's weak 3-flow conjecture
classifications.attribution: paper authors (arXiv:1701.03366, Additive bases and flows in graphs)
classifications.clean_statement: There is a constant k such that every directed graph that is k-edge-connected (as an undirected multigraph) admits a Z_5-antisymmetric flow: a nowhere-zero Z_5-flow phi (assignment of values in Z_5 to arcs respecting Kirchhoff conservation at every vertex) that is moreover antisymmetric, meaning that for the induced flow there is no pair of arcs/values forcing phi(e) = -phi(e') in a way that violates the antisymmetry condition (Jaeger's antisymmetric-flow notion: an orientation/flow such that no two arcs receive opposite values along a closed structure). This is the antisymmetric analogue of Jaeger's weak 3-flow / circular flow program over Z_5.
classifications.rocq_sketch: exists k:nat, forall (D:DiGraph) (Hk: edge_connected D k), exists phi: arc D -> 'Z_5, is_nz_flow phi /\ antisymmetric phi.

classifications.title: Problem 3 (tournaments: large domination number forces S_i)
classifications.attribution: paper authors (arXiv:1702.01607, Coloring tournaments: from local to global; Harutyunyan-Le-Thomasse-Wu style)
classifications.clean_statement: For every integer i >= 1 there exists f(i) such that every tournament T whose domination number dom(T) is at least f(i) contains an isomorphic copy of S_i as a subtournament, where S_i is the i-th iterated/specified tournament (S_1 a single vertex; S_i built recursively, the canonical hard-to-dominate tournament family used in the local-to-global colouring program). Equivalently, tournaments with bounded domination number forbid S_i for some i, so unbounded domination number forces every fixed S_i.
classifications.rocq_sketch: forall i, exists fi, forall (T:Tournament), fi <= domnum T -> exists (S: sub_tournament T), dgiso S (S_fam i).

classifications.title: Problem 4 (large domination -> bounded-size subtournament with large domination)
classifications.attribution: paper authors (arXiv:1702.01607, Coloring tournaments: from local to global)
classifications.clean_statement: For every integer k >= 1 there exist integers K and l such that every tournament T with domination number dom(T) >= K contains a subtournament on exactly l vertices whose own domination number is at least k. (A 'local witness' of high domination compressed into a bounded-size induced subtournament.)
classifications.rocq_sketch: forall k, exists K l, forall (T:Tournament), K <= domnum T -> exists (S: sub_tournament T), #|S| = l /\ k <= domnum S.

classifications.title: Conjecture 3 (chi bounded under orthogonal spaghetti-tree + path decompositions)
classifications.attribution: paper authors (arXiv:1703.07871, Burling graphs, chromatic number, and orthogonal tree-decompositions)
classifications.clean_statement: There exists a function f: N -> N such that for every k >= 1, every (undirected) graph G that simultaneously admits a spaghetti tree-decomposition (T, {B_t}) and a path-decomposition (P, {B_p}) which are k-orthogonal — meaning |B_t intersect B_p| <= k for every t in V(T) and p in V(P) — satisfies chi(G) <= f(k). I.e. bounded pairwise bag-overlap between a spaghetti-tree-decomposition and a path-decomposition implies bounded chromatic number.
classifications.rocq_sketch: exists f, forall k (G:sgraph), spaghetti_treedec G td -> pathdec G pd -> orthogonal k td pd -> chi G <= f k.

classifications.title: Conjecture 3.5 (polynomial domination bound for directed-triangle-free digraphs)
classifications.attribution: paper authors (arXiv:1708.00423, Domination and fractional domination in digraphs)
classifications.clean_statement: There is an integer l such that for every directed-triangle-free digraph D (no directed cycle of length 3) with independence number alpha(D) = alpha, the domination number gamma(D) is at most alpha^l. (Polynomial-in-alpha domination for triangle-free-digraphs; here a dominating set is a set X such that every vertex outside X has an in-neighbour in X, the standard digraph out-domination.)
definitions_needed: independence number alpha of a digraph
domination number gamma of a digraph
classifications.rocq_sketch: exists l, forall (D:DiGraph), no_directed_triangle D -> domnum D <= (alpha D)^l.

classifications.title: Conjecture 4 (short properly-coloured / rainbow-incident cycles, n colours)
classifications.attribution: paper authors (arXiv:1806.00825, Short rainbow cycles in graphs and matroids; Aharoni-type)
classifications.clean_statement: Let G be a simple n-vertex (undirected) graph and c a colouring of E(G) with exactly n colours such that each colour class has size at least r. Then (G,c) contains a cycle C of length at most ceil(n/r) that is properly edge-coloured, i.e. no two incident (consecutive) edges of C receive the same colour. (A strengthening of the Caccetta-Haggkvist-flavoured rainbow-cycle conjecture: n colour classes each of size >= r force a short non-monochromatic-consecutive cycle.)
definitions_needed: edge colouring with n colours, classes of size >= r
properly-coloured cycle (no two incident edges same colour)
classifications.rocq_sketch: forall (G:sgraph)(n r:nat)(c: edge G -> 'I_n), n = #|V G| -> (forall j, r <= #|colorclass c j|) -> exists C, cycle G C /\ length C <= n %/ r + (n %% r != 0) /\ properly_coloured c C.

classifications.title: Conjecture 1.2 (bipartite out-degree threshold forces girth <= 2k)
classifications.attribution: paper authors (arXiv:1809.08324, Short directed cycles in bipartite digraphs; bipartite Caccetta-Haggkvist)
classifications.clean_statement: For every integer k >= 1, if D is a bipartite digraph with n > 0 vertices in each part and every vertex has out-degree strictly greater than n/(k+1), then D has (directed) girth at most 2k, i.e. D contains a directed cycle of length at most 2k. This is the bipartite analogue of the Caccetta-Haggkvist conjecture.
definitions_needed: bipartite digraph (balanced parts of size n)
classifications.rocq_sketch: forall k (D:DiGraph) n, bipartite_balanced D n -> (forall v, n %/ (k+1) < outdeg D v) -> exists c, dicycle D c /\ length c <= 2*k.

classifications.title: Conjecture 1.5 (two-sided out-degree density forces girth <= 2k)
classifications.attribution: paper authors (arXiv:1809.08324, Short directed cycles in bipartite digraphs)
classifications.clean_statement: For every integer k >= 1 and every pair of reals alpha, beta > 0 with k*alpha + beta > 1, if D is a non-null bipartite digraph with bipartition (A,B) where every vertex in A has out-degree at least beta*|B| and every vertex in B has out-degree at least alpha*|A|, then D has directed girth at most 2k (a directed cycle of length <= 2k). Generalises Conjecture 1.2 to unbalanced parts and asymmetric density thresholds.
classifications.rocq_sketch: forall k (a b:R), 0<a -> 0<b -> k*a+b>1 -> forall (D:DiGraph) A B, bipartition D A B -> (forall v, v\in A -> b*#|B| <= outdeg D v) -> (forall v, v\in B -> a*#|A| <= outdeg D v) -> exists c, dicycle D c /\ length c <= 2*k.

classifications.title: Problem 2.15 (complexity of circular F-colouring)
classifications.attribution: paper authors (arXiv:1812.02420, On the Complexity of Digraph Colourings and Vertex Arboricity)
classifications.clean_statement: Let F be a fixed (multi-)digraph. Problem (Circular-F-COL): given an input (multi-)digraph D, decide whether D is circularly F-colourable, i.e. whether there is a homomorphism from D to F respecting the circular-colouring/F-colouring semantics defined in the paper (a generalisation of digraph homomorphism / acyclic-homomorphism colouring). The open question is to classify the computational complexity of this decision problem as a function of the fixed template F.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Definition CircFCol (F D:DiGraph) : Prop := exists h: V D -> V F, circ_F_hom F D h. (* complexity classification: not a single Prop *)

classifications.title: Conjecture 2.16 (circular F-colouring NP-complete when F has a directed cycle)
classifications.attribution: paper authors (arXiv:1812.02420, On the Complexity of Digraph Colourings and Vertex Arboricity)
classifications.clean_statement: Let F be a (multi-)digraph that contains a directed cycle. Then the circular F-colouring decision problem (given D, decide whether D is circularly F-colourable) is NP-complete. (A dichotomy-style hardness conjecture: presence of a directed cycle in the template F is sufficient for NP-completeness.)
classifications.duplicate_of: Problem 2.15 same paper (specific complexity claim within that classification)
definitions_needed: multi-digraph F with a directed cycle
classifications.rocq_sketch: forall (F:DiGraph), (exists c, dicycle F c) -> NPcomplete (CircFCol F).

classifications.title: Problem 3.21 (complexity of fractional dichromatic number <= p)
classifications.attribution: paper authors (arXiv:1812.02420, On the Complexity of Digraph Colourings and Vertex Arboricity)
classifications.clean_statement: Let p >= 1 be a fixed real number. Problem: given a (multi-)digraph D, decide whether the fractional dichromatic number chi-vec_f(D) <= p, where the (fractional) dichromatic number is the (LP-relaxation of the) minimum number of colours needed to partition V(D) into acyclic-induced classes (each class inducing no directed cycle). The open question is the complexity of this threshold decision as a function of fixed p.
definitions_needed: decision problem chi-vec_f <= p for fixed real p
classifications.rocq_sketch: forall (p:R), 1<=p -> decidable_problem (fun D:DiGraph => fracdichromatic D <= p). (* complexity of this is the open question *)

classifications.title: Problem 5.40 (acyclicity = intersecting-family digraph K(k,b))
classifications.attribution: paper authors (arXiv:1812.02420, On the Complexity of Digraph Colourings and Vertex Arboricity)
classifications.clean_statement: For given k,b in N with k >= b, does there exist a digraph K(k,b) on vertex set the b-subsets of [k] (vertex set = binom([k],b)) such that for every subfamily {B_1,...,B_l} of b-subsets, the subdigraph of K(k,b) induced by {B_1,...,B_l} is acyclic if and only if the B_i have a common element (intersection_i B_i nonempty)? I.e. an orientation of the Kneser-type set system whose induced acyclicity exactly encodes the intersecting (helly/star) property.
definitions_needed: common-intersection of a family of sets
classifications.rocq_sketch: forall k b, b<=k -> exists (K: DiGraph) (V K = {set: b.-subsets [k]}), forall (S: {set V K}), acyclic (induced_digraph K S) <-> (\bigcap_(B in S) B != set0).

classifications.title: Question 1 (largest minor-closed class with dichromatic number <= 2)
classifications.attribution: paper authors (arXiv:1907.00351, A Note on Graphs of Dichromatic Number 2)
classifications.clean_statement: What is the largest minor-closed class G_2 of undirected graphs such that every orientation of every graph in G_2 has dichromatic number at most 2? (Equivalently, characterise the maximal minor-closed family whose members, under every orientation, can have their vertices 2-coloured with each colour class inducing an acyclic subdigraph.) This is an open-ended classification question.
classifications.rocq_sketch: (* open-ended: characterise maximal minor-closed C with forall G in C, forall orientation D of G, dichromatic D <= 2 *)

classifications.title: Problem 1.1 (f(k): chromatic threshold forcing acyclic k-chromatic subgraph)
classifications.attribution: paper authors (arXiv:1912.07722, Acyclic subgraphs of tournaments with high chromatic number)
classifications.clean_statement: Determine the minimum integer f(k) such that every oriented graph (in particular every tournament) with dichromatic number at least f(k) contains an acyclic subdigraph with chromatic number at least k. Here 'acyclic subgraph' means a subdigraph inducing no directed cycle, and its chromatic number is that of its underlying undirected graph; the quantity g(k) (the inverse extremal function) is the focus of the companion conjecture.
definitions_needed: chromatic number of an oriented graph (dichromatic number)
(undirected) chromatic number of the acyclic subgraph
classifications.rocq_sketch: Definition f (k:nat) := minn (fun m => forall (G:Oriented), m <= dichromatic G -> exists H, acyclic_sub G H /\ k <= chi (underlying H)).

classifications.title: Conjecture 4.1 (tournaments have acyclic subgraph of chromatic number ~ n^{3/4})
classifications.attribution: paper authors (arXiv:1912.07722, Acyclic subgraphs of tournaments with high chromatic number)
classifications.clean_statement: Every n-vertex tournament has an acyclic subdigraph (a transitive-orderable / directed-cycle-free subdigraph) whose underlying undirected chromatic number is at least n^{3/4 - o(1)}. Equivalently the extremal function g(k) (minimum n forcing acyclic chromatic number k) satisfies g(k) <= k^{4/3 + o(1)}.
classifications.duplicate_of: Problem 1.1 same paper (quantitative form of f/g)
classifications.rocq_sketch: (* asymptotic: forall eps>0, exists n0, forall (T:Tournament) n, n0<=n -> #|T|=n -> exists H, acyclic_sub T H /\ chi (underlying H) >= n powR (3/4 - eps) *)

classifications.title: Conjecture 1.10 (exponentially many non-conflicting group flows on 3-edge-connected oriented graphs)
classifications.attribution: paper authors (arXiv:2005.09767, Many flows in the group connectivity setting)
classifications.clean_statement: There exists a fixed constant c > 1 such that: for every 3-edge-connected oriented graph G = (V,E) of order n, every abelian group Gamma with |Gamma| >= 6, and every prescription f: E -> Gamma, there exist at least c^n nowhere-equal flows phi: E -> Gamma (Gamma-flows satisfying conservation) with phi(e) != f(e) for every edge e. (An exponential lower bound on the number of group-connectivity flows avoiding a forbidden assignment.)
classifications.rocq_sketch: exists c:R, 1<c /\ forall (G:Oriented) (Gam:finGroupType) (f: arc G -> Gam), edge_connected G 3 -> 6 <= #|Gam| -> (c ^ #|V G|) <= #|[set phi | is_flow phi & forall e, phi e != f e]|.

classifications.title: Problem 1.2
classifications.clean_statement: Characterize the finite sets F of digraphs for which the class Forb_ind(F) (all digraphs containing no member of F as an induced subdigraph) has bounded dichromatic number, i.e. there exists a constant c with chi-vec(D) <= c for every D in Forb_ind(F). (Digraph analogue of the Gyarfas-Sumner conjecture; chi-vec is the dichromatic number = min number of colour classes each inducing an acyclic subdigraph.)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Definition heroic (F : seq digraph) := exists c, forall D, (forall H, H \in F -> ~ ind_contains D H) -> dichromatic D <= c.

classifications.title: Conjecture 4.2
classifications.clean_statement: Let H be a hero (a tournament such that {bidirected-K2, H}-free digraphs have bounded dichromatic number) and let F be an oriented forest. Then {bidirected-K2, H, F} is heroic (i.e. Forb_ind of this set has bounded dichromatic number) if and only if either F is a disjoint union of oriented stars, or H is a transitive tournament.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture: forall H F, hero H -> oriented_forest F -> (heroic [:: biK2; H; F] <-> (is_star_forest F \/ transitive_tournament H)).

classifications.title: Conjecture 4.4
classifications.clean_statement: For every oriented forest F and every integer k, the set {bidirected-K2, transitive-tournament-K_k, F} is heroic, i.e. the class of digraphs with no bidirected K2, no transitive tournament on k vertices, and no induced copy of F has bounded dichromatic number. (Here K_k denotes the transitive tournament TT_k.)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture: forall F k, oriented_forest F -> heroic [:: biK2; TT k; F].

classifications.title: Research direction: minimal heroic tournament families
classifications.clean_statement: Characterize all finite and (inclusion-)minimal families H = {H_1,...,H_k} of tournaments such that {directed-K2 (single arc), bidirected-K2, H_1,...,H_k} is a heroic set, i.e. the class of digraphs forbidding all these as induced subdigraphs has bounded dichromatic number. (Forbidding both directed and bidirected K2 forces the class to be oriented graphs without digons.)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Open: classify minimal seq of tournaments Hs with heroic ([:: dK2; biK2] ++ Hs).

classifications.title: Open Problem: exact constant in the exponent
classifications.clean_statement: Determine the exact value of the constant c in the exponent governing powers of paths in tournaments: every tournament on n vertices contains the k-th power of a directed path on roughly n/2^{ck+o(k)} vertices. The proof yields a lower bound with c approximately 3.9, conjectured not to be optimal; find the correct constant.
classifications.attribution: Draganic, Dross, Fox, Girao, Havet, Korandi, Kwan, Narayanan, Postle, Scott et al. (powers of paths in tournaments)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Open asymptotic: optimal c in n / 2^{c k + o(k)} bound for k-th power of directed path in any n-tournament.

classifications.title: Problem 1.4
classifications.clean_statement: Decide the computational complexity of: given an oriented matroid M (vector M), is there a directed circuit of even cardinality (even size) in M? This generalizes the even-cycle problem in digraphs to oriented matroids.
classifications.attribution: paper authors (Even Circuits in Oriented Matroids)
definitions_needed: directed circuit in an oriented matroid
classifications.rocq_sketch: Decision problem (no clean digraph model): exists directed circuit of even size in oriented matroid M.

classifications.title: Problem 4.4
classifications.clean_statement: Determine the computational complexity of Sigma-k-Dicolourability for k in {4,5} and Sigma a surface different from the sphere: given a digraph embedded on (or embeddable in) surface Sigma, decide whether its dichromatic number is at most k.
classifications.attribution: Aboulker, Havet, Knauer, Rambaud et al. (dichromatic number of surfaces)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Complexity of deciding dichromatic D <= k for D on surface Sigma, k in {4,5} (open).

classifications.title: Open Complexity Problem (2-F-PFC for oriented trees of maximum degree >= 3)
classifications.clean_statement: Determine the complexity of 2-F-PFC (the problem of 2-colouring the vertices of an oriented planar graph so that no monochromatic copy of the fixed oriented graph F appears, i.e. Proper-Forbidden-Colouring / monochromatic-subgraph-avoiding colouring) when the underlying undirected tree of the oriented tree F has maximum degree at least 3. In particular, settle the case where iteratively deleting all leaves of F yields a star K_{1,n}.
classifications.attribution: paper authors (Colorings of oriented planar graphs avoiding a monochromatic subgraph)
definitions_needed: oriented tree / underlying tree maximum degree
classifications.rocq_sketch: Complexity of 2-colouring oriented planar G avoiding monochromatic F (oriented tree, max-deg>=3) — open.

classifications.title: Open problem on parameters governing r_1(H)
classifications.clean_statement: Identify natural digraph parameters (analogues of degeneracy and vertex count for undirected graphs) that determine the growth order of the 1-colour oriented Ramsey number r_1(H) for acyclic digraphs H. In particular, decide whether r_1(H) is polynomial or super-polynomial in n = |V(H)| when H has bounded maximum degree.
classifications.attribution: Fox, He, Wigderson et al. (Ramsey numbers of sparse digraphs)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Open asymptotics: growth order of oriented Ramsey r_1(H) for bounded-degree acyclic H.

classifications.title: Question 3.2
classifications.clean_statement: Is there a function f : N -> N such that every digraph D with no induced directed cycle of odd length satisfies chi-vec(D) <= f(omega(D)), where chi-vec is the dichromatic number and omega(D) is the clique number of the underlying undirected graph? (A chi-boundedness-type question for digraphs with no induced odd directed cycle.)
classifications.attribution: Carbonero, Hompe, Moore, Spirkl (counterexample to a triangle-free chi-boundedness conjecture)
definitions_needed: induced directed cycle of odd length
classifications.rocq_sketch: exists f, forall D, (forall C, induced_dicycle D C -> ~~ odd (length C)) -> dichromatic D <= f (omega (underlying D)).

classifications.title: Open problem on construction size
classifications.clean_statement: For the class C_3 of chordal directed graphs, determine whether the size of the smallest (k+1)-dichromatic example in C_3 can be reduced below the current bound n^{2^{poly(n)}}, ideally toward the 2^{poly} bound achieved by Zykov's construction for triangle-free graphs. (Establishes chordal digraphs are not chi-bounded; asks for the optimal blow-up size.)
classifications.attribution: Aboulker, Bousquet, de Verclos et al. (Chordal directed graphs are not chi-bounded)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Open: minimal |V| of (k+1)-dichromatic chordal digraph; lower the n^{2^poly(n)} bound.

classifications.title: Question 1.4
classifications.clean_statement: Is Delta(1,2,2) (the orientation of a triangle obtained by blowing up the three vertices into transitive paths/parts of sizes 1, 2, 2 — a specific oriented graph) a hero in the class of oriented complete multipartite graphs, i.e. is the dichromatic number of Delta(1,2,2)-free oriented complete multipartite graphs bounded by a constant?
classifications.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit et al. (Heroes in oriented complete multipartite graphs)
classifications.rocq_sketch: exists c, forall D, complete_multipartite_oriented D -> ~ ind_contains D (Delta 1 2 2) -> dichromatic D <= c.

classifications.title: Question 5.4
classifications.clean_statement: Let H and F be digraphs such that Delta(1,1,H) (a specific 'blow-up'/join construction over H) is a hero in Forb_ind(F) and H is a hero in Forb_ind(K_1 + F) (where K_1 + F denotes adding a dominating/universal vertex to F). Is it then true that Delta(1,1,H) is a hero in Forb_ind(K_1 + F)? (An inductive transfer/lifting question for heroes across the join operation.)
classifications.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit et al. (Heroes in oriented complete multipartite graphs)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture: hero (Delta 1 1 H) (Forb_ind F) -> hero H (Forb_ind (K1join F)) -> hero (Delta 1 1 H) (Forb_ind (K1join F)).

classifications.title: Conjecture 1.5
classifications.clean_statement: A tournament T has the strong Erdos-Hajnal property if and only if it admits a vertex numbering (linear order) for which the backedge graph (the undirected graph whose edges are the pairs reversed relative to the order) is a forest. (Equivalently: strong EH holds iff some ordering has acyclic/forest backedge graph.)
classifications.attribution: Chudnovsky, Scott, Seymour, Spirkl (Pure pairs X)
definitions_needed: backedge graph of an ordered tournament
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture: forall T:tournament, strongEH T <-> exists (s:{perm}), is_forest (backedge_graph T s).

classifications.title: Informal question: can the D5-freeness hypothesis in Theorem 1.6 be dropped?
classifications.clean_statement: In Theorem 1.6 of Pure Pairs X, the conclusion is proved under the extra hypothesis that the tournament H is D5-free (contains no copy of the specific 5-vertex tournament D5). The open question: can the D5-freeness condition be omitted, i.e. does the conclusion (the strong EH-type statement) hold for all H without assuming D5-freeness?
classifications.attribution: Chudnovsky, Scott, Seymour, Spirkl (Pure pairs X)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Open: does Thm 1.6 conclusion hold without the D5-free hypothesis on H.

classifications.title: Conjecture on r-free digraphs with r > 2n/3
classifications.clean_statement: Every r-free digraph on n vertices with r > 2n/3 is at most one arc away from being acyclic; i.e. it has feedback arc set of size at most 1 (deleting some single arc makes it acyclic). Here 'r-free' means the digraph contains no directed cycle of length at most r (girth greater than r).
classifications.attribution: paper authors (Extremal results on feedback arc sets in digraphs)
definitions_needed: r-free digraph (no directed cycle of length <= r) / directed girth
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture: forall D n, #|V D| = n -> digirth D > 2*n/3 -> exists a, acyclic (del_arc D a).

classifications.title: Suspected directed surplus of a random B-free orientation (Remark 1.6)
classifications.clean_statement: Let B be a fixed family of digraphs and let G be an undirected graph on n vertices that is B-free (its orientations avoid every member of B) with m = ex(n, B) edges, the maximum number of edges. For a random orientation of G, the maximum feedback arc set parameter beta(G) (max over orientations of the size of a minimum feedback arc set, i.e. arcs removed to make it acyclic) is conjectured to satisfy beta(G) = m/2 - Omega(sqrt(m*n)) with high probability. Here beta measures how far an orientation can be from acyclic; the surplus m/2 - beta is conjectured to be of order at least sqrt(mn).
definitions_needed: feedback-arc-set size beta(G) = max over orientations of min FAS
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture surplus_random_orient : forall (B:family) n, let m := ex n B in beta n B = m/2 - Omega (sqrt (m*n)).
classifications.attribution: paper authors (arXiv:2204.01938, Extremal results on feedback arc sets in digraphs)

classifications.title: Open problem on happy triples for l < k/2
classifications.clean_statement: In the context of Aharoni's rainbow generalization of the Caccetta-Häggkvist conjecture (triangle case), determine the best upper bound on the number of 'happy triples' in a graph with k edges and maximum degree at most l, in the regime l < k/2. A 'happy triple' here is a triple of edges that can be used to form a rainbow triangle in the associated edge-colouring setting. The statement is an open-ended request for the optimal function f(k,l) bounding the number of happy triples when l < k/2 (the complementary regime l >= k/2 being the one previously handled).
definitions_needed: graph with k edges and max degree <= l
classifications.rocq_sketch: Question best_bound_happy_triples : forall k l, l < k/2 -> max (#happy_triples G | edges G = k /\ Delta G <= l) = ?.

classifications.title: Question 6.1 (Alon-Tarsi spanning orientation with high out-degree)
classifications.clean_statement: Does every (undirected) graph G have a spanning subgraph H such that H admits an Alon-Tarsi orientation (an orientation in which the number of even-edge Eulerian sub-digraphs differs from the number of odd-edge ones, equivalently the orientation witnesses the Alon-Tarsi list-colouring criterion) and in which every vertex v has out-degree at least (1/2)(deg_G(v) - 1)?
definitions_needed: Alon-Tarsi orientation (even minus odd Eulerian subdigraph count)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Question AT_spanning_orientation : forall G:graph, exists (H:spanning_subgraph G) (o:orientation H), alon_tarsi o /\ forall v, outdeg o v >= (deg G v - 1)/2.

classifications.title: Open problem: closing the gap for d(s)
classifications.clean_statement: Let d(s) be the largest integer such that every digraph D on 2n vertices with minimum out-degree at least s contains an induced subdigraph on n vertices with minimum out-degree at least d(s) (for all n). Known bounds give Omega(log s) <= s/2 - d(s) <= O(sqrt(s log s)). The open problem is to close the gap, i.e. determine the asymptotic order of s/2 - d(s).
definitions_needed: d(s) = largest int s.t. half-size subdigraph keeps min out-degree d(s)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Question d_of_s_gap : exists f, s/2 - d s = Theta (f s) /\ Omega (log s) <= s/2 - d s <= O (sqrt (s * log s)).
classifications.attribution: paper authors (arXiv:2210.12699, Subdigraphs of prescribed size and outdegree)

classifications.title: Conjecture 1.4 (directed Sidorenko for bipartite oriented graphs with hom to vec K_2)
classifications.clean_statement: If B is a bipartite oriented graph admitting a homomorphism B -> vec{K_2} (the directed edge, i.e. B is consistently 2-orientable so all arcs go from one part to the other), then B has the directed Sidorenko property: for every directed graphon / target W, the homomorphism density t(B, W) is at least the product over arcs of the appropriate edge densities, i.e. t(B,W) >= t(vec{K_2}, W)^{e(B)}.
definitions_needed: homomorphism density t(B,W) in a digraphon
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture dir_sidorenko : forall B, bipartite_oriented B -> hom_exists B vecK2 -> forall W, t B W >= (t vecK2 W) ^ (e B).

classifications.title: Conjecture 1.7 (directed forcing for oriented graphs with hom to an edge and a cycle)
classifications.clean_statement: If B is an oriented graph admitting a homomorphism to a single directed edge (vec{K_2}) and whose underlying undirected graph contains a cycle, then B has the directed forcing property: any directed graphon W with t(B,W) = t(vec{K_2},W)^{e(B)} (the Sidorenko bound attained) must be quasirandom in the directed sense.
definitions_needed: underlying undirected graph has a cycle
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture dir_forcing : forall B, hom_exists B vecK2 -> has_cycle (underlying B) -> forcing_property B.

classifications.title: Equivalence of asymmetric and classical Sidorenko properties
classifications.clean_statement: For bipartite graphs, the asymmetric Sidorenko property (an inequality t(H, W) >= prod over edges of distinct one-sided densities, with the two parts treated asymmetrically) is conjectured to be equivalent to the classical Sidorenko property (t(H,W) >= t(K_2,W)^{e(H)} for all graphons W). I.e. a bipartite graph H has the asymmetric Sidorenko property iff it has the classical Sidorenko property.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture asym_eq_classical_sidorenko : forall H, bipartite H -> (asym_sidorenko H <-> classical_sidorenko H).

classifications.title: Problem 1 (value of r_t)
classifications.clean_statement: Determine the value of r_t, the extremal/threshold constant defined in the temporalization-via-balanced-bi-trees framework of the paper: r_t is (per the paper's setup) the optimal ratio/threshold governing the size of the largest temporal structure (balanced bi-tree) guaranteeably extractable. Without the paper's precise definition the constant cannot be pinned down further; the problem asks for its exact value.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Question value_rt : r_t = ?.
classifications.attribution: paper authors (arXiv:2304.03567, Temporalizing digraphs via linear-size balanced bi-trees)

classifications.title: Problem 2 (max c_b for linear-size balanced bi-tree in strong digraphs)
classifications.clean_statement: Determine the largest constant c_b such that every strongly connected digraph on n vertices contains a balanced bi-tree of size at least c_b * n. A bi-tree is a pair of arc/vertex-disjoint out- and in-branchings (an out-tree and an in-tree) rooted appropriately; 'balanced' constrains the two trees to comparable size. The problem asks for the optimal linear ratio c_b.
definitions_needed: largest constant c_b with c_b*n guarantee
classifications.rocq_sketch: Question max_cb : sup { c | forall D, strong D -> exists T, bitree T D /\ size T >= c * n D } = ?.

classifications.title: Problem 3 (subquadratic left-maximal dfs-tree)
classifications.clean_statement: Can a left-maximal DFS tree be computed in o(n^2) time in minimally strongly connected digraphs (digraphs that are strong but lose strong connectivity upon removing any arc)? More generally, what is the time complexity of computing a left-maximal DFS tree in arbitrary digraphs? This is an algorithmic complexity question about the cost of constructing a particular DFS tree.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Question dfs_subquadratic : exists alg, computes_left_maximal_dfs alg /\ time alg = o (n^2) on min_strong_digraphs.

classifications.title: Conjecture 1 (O(log n) forward cover of strong digraphs)
classifications.clean_statement: Every strongly connected digraph D on n vertices has a forward cover of size O(log n): there is a collection of O(log n) 'forward' structures (forward-reachable paths/trees from chosen sources following the temporalization order) whose union covers all vertices (and the relevant temporal-reachability requirement). The conjecture asserts a logarithmic upper bound on the minimum forward-cover size.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture forward_cover_log : forall D, strong D -> exists C, forward_cover C D /\ size C <= O (log (n D)).

classifications.title: Conjecture (Section 9, characterization of 2-extremal digraphs)
classifications.clean_statement: Aboulker et al. Conjecture 9.2: a digraph D is 2-extremal (it has dichromatic number / arc-connectivity extremality at the value 2, i.e. D meets the relevant arc-connectivity-vs-dichromatic bound with parameter k=2) if and only if D belongs to the explicitly described class H_2 (built by tree-join / substitution operations from base members). Equivalently, the family of 2-extremal digraphs is exactly H_2. (Reconstructed from arXiv:2304.04690; raw extraction was empty.)
definitions_needed: k-extremal digraph (arc-connectivity vs dichromatic number extremal)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture two_extremal_iff_H2 : forall D, two_extremal D <-> in_H2 D.
classifications.attribution: paper authors (Aboulker et al., arXiv:2304.04690, Digraph Colouring and Arc-Connectivity)

classifications.title: Conjecture 1.7 (t(l,k) = l^{k-1}, Flashes and rainbows in tournaments)
classifications.clean_statement: For all positive integers l and k, t(l,k) = l^{k-1}, where t(l,k) is the threshold/extremal function defined in the paper governing the appearance of 'flashes' and rainbow structures in edge-coloured tournaments: t(l,k) is the maximum number of colours (or maximal size) such that an l-bounded, k-parameter colouring of a tournament avoids the rainbow structure, conjectured to equal l^{k-1}.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture t_lk_formula : forall l k, 0 < l -> 0 < k -> t l k = l ^ (k-1).
classifications.attribution: paper authors (arXiv:2305.13422, Flashes and rainbows in tournaments)

classifications.title: Question 10 (high-degeneracy out-neighbourhood for large chromatic number)
classifications.clean_statement: For each integer d, does there exist an integer chi (depending on d) such that for every graph G with chromatic number chi(G) >= chi and every tournament T on the same vertex set V(G), there is a vertex v for which the induced subgraph G[N^+_T(v)] (G restricted to the out-neighbourhood of v in T) has degeneracy at least d?
definitions_needed: out-neighbourhood N^+_T(v) in a tournament on V(G)
G and T share vertex set
classifications.rocq_sketch: Question chi_forces_degenerate_outnbhd : forall d, exists c, forall G T, chi G >= c -> exists v, degeneracy (G[Nout T v]) >= d.
classifications.attribution: paper authors (arXiv:2305.15585, Chromatic number is not tournament-local)

classifications.title: Conjecture 11 (cycle in out-neighbourhood for large chromatic number)
classifications.clean_statement: There is a constant chi_0 such that for every graph G with chi(G) >= chi_0 and every tournament T on the vertex set V(G), there exists a vertex v such that the induced subgraph G[N^+_T(v)] (G restricted to the out-neighbourhood of v in T) contains a cycle (equivalently is not a forest). Restated: sufficiently large chromatic number forces some out-neighbourhood to induce a non-forest.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture chi_forces_cycle_outnbhd : exists c, forall G T, chi G >= c -> exists v, has_cycle (G[Nout T v]).

classifications.title: Conjecture 12 (random tournament forces large clique in out-neighbourhood)
classifications.clean_statement: For every positive integer k there exists chi such that if T is the uniformly random tournament on vertex set [N], then with high probability as N -> infinity, for every graph G on [N] with chi(G) >= chi there is a vertex v in [N] for which the induced subgraph G[N^+_T(v)] has chromatic number (or clique/parameter, as written 'G[N^+_T(v)] >= k') at least k. I.e. for a typical tournament, large chromatic number forces a large-chromatic out-neighbourhood.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture random_tour_forces_chi_outnbhd : forall k, exists c, whp_{T~unif([N])} (forall G, chi G >= c -> exists v, chi (G[Nout T v]) >= k).

classifications.title: Conjecture 13
classifications.clean_statement: There exists a function f(N) = o(log N) such that for every undirected graph G on N vertices with chromatic number chi(G) >= f(N), and every tournament T on the same vertex set V(G), there is a vertex v such that the subgraph of G induced on the out-neighbourhood N_T^+(v) = {u : v->u in T} has chromatic number chi(G[N_T^+(v)]) >= 3. (Roughly: chromatic number is 'not tournament-local' — high chromatic number forces a tournament out-neighbourhood with chromatic number >= 3.)
classifications.attribution: Nguyen, Scott, Seymour (Chromatic number is not tournament-local, arXiv:2305.15585)
definitions_needed: induced undirected subgraph on a vertex set
classifications.rocq_sketch: exists f : nat -> nat, (forall e>0, eventually f N <= e*ln N) /\ forall (G:sgraph) (T:Tournament) (V=V T=V G), chi G >= f #|V| -> exists v, chi (induced G (N_out T v)) >= 3

classifications.title: Question 1.1
classifications.clean_statement: Classify (characterize) the finite families F of digraphs that are vec-chi-finite: a family F is vec-chi-finite if the class of digraphs containing no member of F as an induced subdigraph has bounded dichromatic number, i.e. there is a constant c with vec-chi(D) <= c for every F-induced-free digraph D. (This is the directed/heroes analogue of asking which forbidden induced families bound the dichromatic number.)
classifications.attribution: Aboulker, Charbit, Naserasr et al. (On heroes in digraphs with forbidden induced forests, arXiv:2306.04710)
definitions_needed: vec-chi-finite (bounded dichromatic number over an induced-free class)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Definition vecchi_finite (F:seq DiGraph) := exists c, forall D, Forb_ind_free F D -> dichromatic D <= c. (* classification of such F is open-ended *)

classifications.title: Open problem: hero status of Δ(1,m,m') for degree-4 oriented stars
classifications.clean_statement: For an oriented star F of (total) degree 4, and for the orientation Delta(1,m,m') of a spider/subdivided-claw-type oriented tree with arm parameters 1, m, m' (m,m' >= 1), determine for which integers m,m' >= 1 the family {Delta(1,m,m'), F} is vec-chi-finite, i.e. the class of digraphs forbidding both Delta(1,m,m') and the degree-4 oriented star F as induced subdigraphs has bounded dichromatic number.
classifications.attribution: Aboulker, Charbit, Naserasr et al. (On heroes in digraphs with forbidden induced forests, arXiv:2306.04710)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Definition hero_pair (m m':nat) (F:DiGraph) := exists c, forall D, Forb_ind_free [Delta 1 m m'; F] D -> dichromatic D <= c. (* determine the (m,m') set *)

classifications.title: Question 3.5
classifications.clean_statement: Is it true that for every tournament T there exists a single linear order prec of V(T) that is simultaneously a vec-omega-ordering (an ordering achieving omegabar(T), i.e. the backedge graph under prec has clique number equal to omegabar(T)) and a vec-chi-ordering (an ordering achieving vec-chi(T) via the corresponding coloring/backedge structure)? I.e. one order witnesses both the omegabar-optimal and the dichromatic-optimal value at once.
classifications.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (Clique number of tournaments, arXiv:2310.04265)
classifications.rocq_sketch: forall T:Tournament, exists pi:{perm V T}, is_omegabar_order pi /\ is_dichromatic_order pi

classifications.title: Conjecture 3.12
classifications.clean_statement: For every integer k >= 1, the class of tournaments with twin-width at most k is vec-chi-bounded: there exists a function f_k such that every tournament T with twin-width(T) <= k satisfies vec-chi(T) <= f_k(omegabar(T)) (the dichromatic number is bounded in terms of the omegabar clique number within each bounded-twin-width class).
classifications.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (Clique number of tournaments, arXiv:2310.04265)
classifications.rocq_sketch: forall k, exists f, forall T:Tournament, twinwidth T <= k -> dichromatic T <= f (omegabar T)

classifications.title: Conjecture 3.13
classifications.clean_statement: There exists a function f such that for every tournament T there is an ordering prec* of V(T) with [truncated in source; reconstructed from arXiv:2310.04265]: under prec*, every vertex has back-degree (number of in-neighbours appearing earlier, i.e. backedges) bounded by f(omegabar(T)); equivalently the backedge graph of prec* has bounded degeneracy/maximum degree in terms of omegabar(T), giving a 'good' ordering certifying small dichromatic number.
classifications.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (Clique number of tournaments, arXiv:2310.04265)
definitions_needed: backedge graph and back-degree under an order
classifications.rocq_sketch: exists f, forall T:Tournament, exists pi:{perm V T}, forall v, backdeg pi v <= f (omegabar T)

classifications.title: Conjecture 3.16
classifications.clean_statement: There exists a function f such that for every tournament T there is a BST-ordering prec of T with [truncated in source; reconstructed]: BST = 'bounded-strong-component / binary-search-tree style' ordering in which the backedge structure restricted to each strong piece is controlled, so that vec-chi(T) <= f(omegabar(T)) via this ordering. Faithful reconstruction limited by truncation; the spirit is: a special class of orderings (BST-orderings) always exists certifying the dichromatic number is bounded by a function of omegabar.
classifications.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (Clique number of tournaments, arXiv:2310.04265)
classifications.rocq_sketch: exists f, forall T:Tournament, exists pi:{perm V T}, BST_order pi /\ dichromatic T <= f (omegabar T)

classifications.title: Conjecture 4.3 (Gyárfás-Sumner for Tournaments)
