classifications.clean_statement: A tournament H is vec-chi-binding (heroic / domination-binding: the class of H-free tournaments — tournaments not containing H as a subtournament — has dichromatic number bounded by a function of omegabar, equivalently is vec-chi-bounded) if and only if H has a backedge graph (for some/every vertex ordering) that is a forest. This is the tournament analogue of the Gyárfás-Sumner conjecture.
classifications.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (Clique number of tournaments, arXiv:2310.04265)
classifications.rocq_sketch: forall H:Tournament, vecchi_binding H <-> exists pi, is_forest (backedge_graph H pi)

classifications.title: Conjecture 4.11
classifications.clean_statement: Let (M, prec) be an ordered (undirected) graph with maximum degree 1 (a matching together with a fixed linear order of its vertices). Then the class of (M, prec)-free ordered graphs — ordered graphs containing no copy of (M, prec) as an ordered induced/sub pattern — is chi-bounded: there is a function bounding chromatic number by clique number across the class.
classifications.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (Clique number of tournaments, arXiv:2310.04265)
definitions_needed: chi-bounded (undirected chi vs omega binding function)
classifications.rocq_sketch: forall (M:sgraph) (pi:order) , maxdeg M <= 1 -> exists f, forall G ordered, free_of (M,pi) G -> chi G <= f (omega G)

classifications.title: Conjecture 5.3 (Large dom implies an ω̄-cluster)
classifications.clean_statement: There exist functions f and ell such that for every integer k, every tournament T with domination number dom(T) >= f(k) contains a subtournament X with |X| <= ell(k) and omegabar(X) >= k. (Large domination number forces a small omegabar-cluster: a bounded-size subtournament of clique number >= k.)
classifications.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (Clique number of tournaments, arXiv:2310.04265)
classifications.rocq_sketch: exists f ell, forall k (T:Tournament), domnum T >= f k -> exists X, sub_tournament X T /\ #|X| <= ell k /\ omegabar X >= k

classifications.title: Conjecture 5.6
classifications.clean_statement: There exists a function g such that for every integer t, if T is a tournament in which every vertex v satisfies omegabar(N^+(v)) <= t (the out-neighbourhood subtournament has omegabar at most t), then omegabar(T) <= g(t). (Local boundedness of omegabar on out-neighbourhoods implies global boundedness.)
classifications.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (Clique number of tournaments, arXiv:2310.04265)
classifications.rocq_sketch: exists g, forall t (T:Tournament), (forall v, omegabar (induced T (N_out T v)) <= t) -> omegabar T <= g t

classifications.title: Question 5.9
classifications.clean_statement: Is there a function ell such that for every tournament T and integer k, if omegabar(T) >= k then T has a subtournament A with |A| <= ell(k) and omegabar(A) >= k? (A bounded-size witness for the omegabar value — a 'compact omegabar-cluster'.)
classifications.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (Clique number of tournaments, arXiv:2310.04265)
classifications.rocq_sketch: exists ell, forall k (T:Tournament), omegabar T >= k -> exists A, sub_tournament A T /\ #|A| <= ell k /\ omegabar A >= k

classifications.title: Conjecture 5.10
classifications.clean_statement: For every integer k >= 3, there are infinitely many k-omegabar-critical tournaments: tournaments T with omegabar(T) = k such that every proper subtournament (obtained by deleting at least one vertex) has omegabar < k.
classifications.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (Clique number of tournaments, arXiv:2310.04265)
definitions_needed: k-omegabar-critical tournament (omegabar drops on every vertex deletion)
classifications.rocq_sketch: forall k, 3<=k -> forall N, exists T:Tournament, #|V T| > N /\ omegabar T = k /\ forall v, omegabar (del_tournament T v) < k

classifications.title: Conjecture 6.1
classifications.clean_statement: If a class of digraphs C is vec-chi-bounded (dichromatic number bounded by a function of omegabar over C), then its closure under substitution (the smallest class containing C and closed under substituting digraphs of the class into vertices) is also vec-chi-bounded.
classifications.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (Clique number of tournaments, arXiv:2310.04265)
definitions_needed: closure of a class under substitution
classifications.rocq_sketch: forall C, vecchi_bounded C -> vecchi_bounded (subst_closure C)

classifications.title: Conjecture 1.12
classifications.clean_statement: For every undirected tree, there exists some orientation of it that has the tournament anti-Sidorenko property: an oriented tree T such that, over random tournaments (or in the limit / for all tournaments), the homomorphism density of T is at most the value predicted by the random/quasirandom model — i.e. the count of copies of the oriented tree is anti-Sidorenko (minimized, in the Sidorenko-type density sense, by the quasirandom tournament). Faithful reconstruction limited; precise inequality direction per arXiv:2402.08418.
classifications.attribution: authors of 'Variations on Sidorenko's conjecture in tournaments' (arXiv:2402.08418)
definitions_needed: homomorphism density of an oriented tree into a tournament
tournament anti-Sidorenko property (density inequality vs quasirandom)
classifications.rocq_sketch: forall (Tr:tree), exists (o:orientation Tr), anti_sidorenko_density o  (* hom-density inequality over tournaments, needs reals *)

classifications.title: Conjecture 4.2
classifications.clean_statement: There is a function f such that for every integer k there is a polynomial-time algorithm that, given a tournament T, either correctly concludes omegabar(T) >= k, or finds an ordering prec of V(T) such that the backedge graph T^prec has clique number omega(T^prec) <= f(k). (An efficient approximate forest/clique-ordering: poly-time certify large omegabar or output a near-optimal ordering, even though exactly finding forest-orderings is NP-complete.)
classifications.duplicate_of: = Cheng/path-FAS path_matching_fas folder is distinct; this is the AACL/2402.10782 forest-ordering approximation (arXiv:2402.10782)
classifications.attribution: authors of 'Finding forest-orderings of tournaments is NP-complete' (arXiv:2402.10782)
definitions_needed: backedge graph T^prec and its clique number omega
classifications.rocq_sketch: exists f, forall k, exists (A:poly_alg Tournament), forall T, (A T = inl tt -> omegabar T >= k) /\ (forall pi, A T = inr pi -> omega (backedge_graph T pi) <= f k)

classifications.title: Conjecture 3
classifications.clean_statement: Let a-vec(n) (written $\vec{a}(n)$) denote the minimum acyclic number over all oriented triangle-free graphs of order n, where the acyclic number of a digraph D is the maximum size of an acyclic (i.e. cycle-free) induced subdigraph of D. The conjecture asserts $\vec{a}(n)=\Theta(\sqrt{n\log n})$, i.e. there exist constants c1,c2>0 such that for all sufficiently large n, c1*sqrt(n log n) <= a-vec(n) <= c2*sqrt(n log n).
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture forall n, exists c1 c2, c1 * sqrt(n * ln n) <= acyclic_number n <= c2 * sqrt(n * ln n) (* over oriented triangle-free *)
classifications.attribution: paper authors (Aboulker, Bousquet, de Verclos et al.)

classifications.title: Conjecture 4
classifications.clean_statement: Let t-vec(n) (written $\vec{t}(n)$) denote the maximum dichromatic number over all oriented triangle-free graphs of order n, where the dichromatic number $\vec{\chi}(D)$ is the least k such that V(D) can be partitioned into k sets each inducing an acyclic subdigraph. The conjecture asserts $\vec{t}(n)=\Theta(\sqrt{n/\log n})$, i.e. there exist constants c1,c2>0 with c1*sqrt(n/log n) <= t-vec(n) <= c2*sqrt(n/log n) for all large n.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture forall n, exists c1 c2, c1 * sqrt(n / ln n) <= max_dichromatic_triangle_free n <= c2 * sqrt(n / ln n)
classifications.attribution: paper authors (Aboulker, Bousquet, de Verclos et al.)

classifications.title: Open question on strong 2-kernels in split digraphs
classifications.clean_statement: A split digraph is a digraph whose vertex set partitions into a clique (a set inducing a complete/semicomplete part) and an independent set. A source is a vertex with in-degree 0. A strong k-kernel is a set S of vertices that is independent in the k-th power and such that every vertex outside S can reach S by a directed path of length at most k (k-domination); for k=2 this is a 'distant' / 2-step domination kernel that is also 2-independent. The question asks: does every split digraph G with no sources admit a strong 2-kernel S with |S| <= |V(G)|/2?
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture forall G, split_digraph G -> no_sources G -> exists S, strong2kernel G S /\ #|S| * 2 <= #|V G|

classifications.title: Conjecture 1.2
classifications.clean_statement: Let G be a strongly 2-connected digraph drawn (possibly with crossings) in an annulus Sigma, such that every directed cycle has winding number one around the annulus (each directed cycle encircles the hole exactly once). Then G can be redrawn in Sigma without crossings such that every directed cycle still has winding number one. (A planarity-on-the-annulus / drawing existence statement arising in the structural theory of digraphs in which all directed cycles have length three.)
definitions_needed: winding number of a directed cycle
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture forall G, strong2conn G -> annulus_drawing_wind1 G -> exists D, planar_annulus_drawing D /\ all_dicycles_wind1 D (* topological, needs reals/topology *)

classifications.title: Informal Question (NP-characterization of weightable digraphs)
classifications.clean_statement: A digraph is 'weightable' (in the sense of the paper 'When all directed cycles have length three') if its arcs/vertices admit a weighting certifying the relevant structural property. The (informal) question asks whether there is a polynomial-time-verifiable construction characterizing all weightable digraphs, i.e. an NP-characterization (membership in NP via a polynomially-checkable certificate). The notion of 'weightable' is paper-specific and not fully reconstructible from the truncated statement.
classifications.rocq_sketch: (* informal; no clean statement: 'exists polynomially-verifiable certificate for weightable D' — computation-model dependent *)

classifications.title: Problem 6
classifications.clean_statement: For a positive real alpha, does there exist a polynomial P_alpha such that unvd(D) <= P_alpha(|V(D)|) for every digraph D with maximum average degree at most alpha? Here unvd(D) is the unavoidability number of D: the least N such that every tournament on N vertices contains D as a subdigraph (D is N-unavoidable). 'Maximum average degree' mad(D) = max over subdigraphs H of 2|A(H)|/|V(H)|.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture forall alpha, exists P, forall D, mad D <= alpha -> unvd D <= P (#|V D|)

classifications.title: Conjecture 7
classifications.clean_statement: A family F of digraphs is 'linearly unavoidable' if there is a constant c such that unvd(D) <= c*|V(D)| for every D in F (every tournament on c*|V(D)| vertices contains D). For a family F, F[k] denotes the family of k-blow-ups of members of F (each vertex replaced by an independent set / k copies with arcs lifted). Conjecture: if F is linearly unavoidable and has bounded maximum average degree, then F[k] is also linearly unavoidable.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture (lin_unavoid F /\ bounded_mad F) -> exists c, forall D, blowup_mem F k D -> unvd D <= c * #|V D|

classifications.title: Problem 8
classifications.clean_statement: What is the infimum of all constants C such that, for every large enough integer n, the k-blow-up of every oriented tree of order n is (C^k * k*n)-unavoidable (i.e. contained in every tournament on C^k*k*n vertices)? (Determine the optimal base C of the exponential factor in the unavoidability bound for blow-ups of oriented trees.)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Definition C_opt := inf { C | forall n large, forall T oriented_tree(n), unvd (blowup k T) <= C^k * k * n } (* determine value *)

classifications.title: Conjecture 9
classifications.clean_statement: There exists an absolute constant C such that unvd(D) <= C * unvd(D - v) for every acyclic digraph D and every vertex v of D, where D - v is the digraph obtained by deleting v and unvd is the unavoidability number (least N such that every N-vertex tournament contains the digraph).
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture exists C, forall D, acyclic D -> forall v, unvd D <= C * unvd (del_vertex D v)

classifications.title: Conjecture 10
classifications.clean_statement: There is an absolute constant C such that for every integer k, every k-extension of an oriented tree of order n is C^k*(2n-2)-unavoidable (contained in every tournament on C^k*(2n-2) vertices). A k-extension of a digraph subdivides/extends each arc by k (paper-specific extension operation replacing arcs with directed paths or adding k extra structure), distinct from a blow-up.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture exists C, forall k n T, oriented_tree T n -> unvd (extension k T) <= C^k * (2*n - 2)

classifications.title: Conjecture 11
classifications.clean_statement: If a family F of digraphs is linearly unavoidable (there is c with unvd(D) <= c*|V(D)| for all D in F), then the family of k-extensions of digraphs in F is also linearly unavoidable.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture lin_unavoid F -> exists c, forall D, ext_mem F k D -> unvd D <= c * #|V D|

classifications.title: Problem 12
classifications.clean_statement: What is the minimum function f such that every k-extension of every forest (disjoint union of oriented trees) of order n is (2^{f(k)} * n)-unavoidable (contained in every tournament on 2^{f(k)}*n vertices)? (Determine the optimal growth of the exponent f(k).)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Definition f_min := min f s.t. forall k n F, forest F n -> unvd (extension k F) <= 2^(f k) * n (* determine f *)

classifications.title: Question 1.1
classifications.clean_statement: For which fixed k > 0 does there exist a bound c such that every simple (undirected) graph G either contains the odd cycle C_{2k+1} as a subgraph or has chromatic number chi(G) <= c (or both)? (As stated this is for graphs; in the digraph context of the paper it is the analogue: high chromatic number forces an odd cycle. Note: for undirected graphs the answer is that no finite c works since complete graphs K_n have no C_{2k+1} for k>=2 yet unbounded chi — so the intended question is the digraph/oriented version with the additional minimum-out-degree hypothesis, see Question 1.2.) The 'epsilon' parameter refers to the minimum out-degree fraction in the digraph setting.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Question characterize {(eps,k) | exists c, forall G, contains_odd_cycle G k \/ chi G <= c }

classifications.title: Question 1.2
classifications.clean_statement: Which orientations of cycles are contained as a subdigraph in every digraph D with both high minimum out-degree (>= epsilon|V(D)| for fixed epsilon>0) and high chromatic number chi(D)>=c? I.e. characterize the orientations of the cycle C_m that are unavoidable under simultaneous high min-out-degree and high (di)chromatic number. (Background: directed cycles are forced by high dichromatic number; the question is about general orientations of cycles.)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Question characterize { cyclic_orientation O | exists c, forall D, min_outdeg D >= eps*#|V D| -> chi D >= c -> O subdigraph_of D }

classifications.title: Question 4.1
classifications.clean_statement: For which fixed digraphs H does there exist, for every epsilon>0, a constant c_epsilon >= 1 such that every loopless digraph D without parallel arcs with chromatic number chi(D) >= c_epsilon and minimum out-degree at least epsilon*|V(D)| contains H as a subdigraph? (Characterize the digraphs H forced by high chromatic number together with linear minimum out-degree.)
definitions_needed: chromatic number chi of a digraph
classifications.rocq_sketch: Question characterize { H | forall eps>0, exists c, forall D, simple D -> chi D >= c -> min_outdeg D >= eps*#|V D| -> H subdigraph_of D }

classifications.title: Question 4.2
classifications.clean_statement: For a fixed digraph H, what is the minimum value epsilon_H >= 0 such that there exists a constant c >= 1 with: every loopless digraph D without parallel arcs with chromatic number chi(D) >= c and minimum out-degree at least epsilon_H*|V(D)| contains H as a subdigraph? (Determine the optimal out-degree threshold epsilon_H for each H.)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Definition eps_H H := inf { eps | exists c, forall D, simple D -> chi D >= c -> min_outdeg D >= eps*#|V D| -> H subdigraph_of D }

classifications.title: Question 5.1
classifications.clean_statement: Let G be an oriented graph (a digraph with no digon). The minimum semidegree is delta^0(G) = min over all vertices v of min(outdeg(v), indeg(v)). Conjecture: every oriented graph G with delta^0(G) >= k/2 contains, as a subdigraph, every orientation of the path on k edges (the k-edge path P_{k+1}) EXCEPT the antidirected orientations (those whose every internal vertex is a source or a sink). Equivalently, k/2 minimum semidegree forces all non-antidirected orientations of the k-edge path.
definitions_needed: orientation of a path (sequence of forward/backward arcs)
antidirected orientation (all internal vertices are sources/sinks)
subdigraph containment of an oriented path
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture q51 (G : oriented) (k : nat) : (forall v, k <= 2 * minn (outdeg G v) (indeg G v)) -> forall (P : pathorient k), ~ antidirected P -> contains_subdigraph G (path_of P).

classifications.title: Problem 23
classifications.clean_statement: Characterize the finite tournaments H for which there exists a countable tournament G that is H-free (contains no copy of H as a subtournament) but is induced-saturated under single-arc reversal: reversing the direction of any one arc of G creates a copy of H. I.e. determine the set of finite H admitting such an arc-flip-saturated countable H-free tournament.
classifications.rocq_sketch: Definition flipsat_Tfree (H : finTournament) (G : countableTournament) := Tfree H G /\ forall a : arc G, contains_sub H (flip_arc G a). (* characterize {H | exists G, flipsat_Tfree H G} *)

classifications.title: Conjecture 24
classifications.clean_statement: Let T be a finite tournament. If T is not transitive, then there exists a countably infinite T-free tournament S_T (no subtournament isomorphic to T) such that every locally finite perturbation of S_T contains an induced copy of T. Here a locally finite perturbation flips the orientation of a set of arcs touching each vertex only finitely often (equivalently each vertex's modified arc-set is finite).
definitions_needed: locally finite perturbation (each vertex flips finitely many arcs)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture c24 (T : finTournament) : ~ transitive T -> exists S : countableTournament, Tfree T S /\ forall p, locally_finite_perturb S p -> contains_induced T (apply_perturb S p).

classifications.title: Conjecture 6.1
classifications.clean_statement: In the directed analogue of Thomassen's avoidability framework (a digraph H is 'avoidable' if there is a constant c such that every strongly connected digraph of minimum out- and in-degree at least c, or sufficiently high connectivity, contains H as a subdigraph fails — i.e. H is avoidable if no degree/connectivity condition forces it; precisely, H is avoidable iff for every k there is a digraph of min semidegree >= k not containing H). Conjecture: every orientation of every odd cycle C_{2m+1} is avoidable.
definitions_needed: avoidable digraph (no minimum-semidegree bound forces containment)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture c61 (m : nat) (o : cycleorient (2*m+1)) : avoidable (oriented_cycle o). (* avoidable H := forall k, exists D : oriented, min_semideg D >= k /\ ~ contains_sub H D *)

classifications.title: Question 6.2
classifications.clean_statement: A digraph D admits a height function if there is a map h : V(D) -> Z (or R) such that for every arc (u,v), h(v) > h(u) (i.e. D is acyclic / topologically orderable; for the avoidability paper 'height function' means a level assignment respecting arc orientations). Question: is every digraph that admits a height function NOT avoidable (i.e. for any such H there is a finite minimum-semidegree threshold forcing every digraph of that semidegree to contain H)?
definitions_needed: height function h : V -> Z with h(v) > h(u) for each arc (u,v) (acyclic level map)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Question q62 : forall (H : oriented), has_height_function H -> ~ avoidable H. (* has_height_function H := exists h : V H -> int, forall a, h (head a) > h (tail a) *)

classifications.title: Question 6.3
classifications.clean_statement: Among the (up to isomorphism) orientations of the 4-cycle C_4 — the directed 4-cycle, the two 'two-block' orientations, and the antidirected/alternating 4-cycle — determine exactly which are avoidable, where H is avoidable iff for every k there exists a digraph of minimum semidegree >= k not containing H as a subdigraph.
definitions_needed: the orientations of C_4 (directed, two-block, antidirected)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Question q63 : forall (o : cycleorient 4), {avoidable (oriented_cycle o)} + {~ avoidable (oriented_cycle o)}. (* classify the C_4 orientations *)

classifications.title: Eulerian-avoidability characterization
classifications.clean_statement: Characterize the digraphs H that are 'Eulerian-avoidable': H is Eulerian-avoidable iff for every k there exists an Eulerian digraph (connected, with outdeg(v) = indeg(v) at every vertex) of minimum semidegree >= k that does not contain H as a subdigraph. Determine the full class of such H.
definitions_needed: Eulerian digraph (connected, indeg = outdeg at each vertex)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Definition eul_avoidable (H : oriented) := forall k, exists D, eulerian D /\ min_semideg D >= k /\ ~ contains_sub H D. (* characterize {H | eul_avoidable H} *)

classifications.title: Question 6.4
classifications.clean_statement: Is every orientation of the 4-cycle C_4 Eulerian-avoidable? I.e. for each orientation o of C_4 and every k, does there exist an Eulerian digraph (indeg = outdeg at every vertex, connected) of minimum semidegree >= k not containing the oriented C_4 given by o?
classifications.rocq_sketch: Question q64 : forall (o : cycleorient 4), eul_avoidable (oriented_cycle o).

classifications.title: Problem 5.1
classifications.clean_statement: For a q-edge-colored tournament, a directed path is 'color-avoiding' (with q-1 colors) if there is a color c such that the path uses no arc of color c (avoids one color). Let f_{q,q-1}(N) denote the guaranteed length (number of vertices) of the longest such color-avoiding directed path over all q-colored N-vertex tournaments achieving the extremal value. Problem: for some integers q >= 4 and N, does there exist a non-transitive q-edge-colored N-vertex tournament whose longest color-avoiding directed path has strictly fewer than f_{q,q-1}(N) vertices? (i.e. can a non-transitive coloring beat / fall below the transitive extremal bound).
definitions_needed: color-avoiding directed path (omits at least one of the q colors)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Problem p51 : exists q N (T : ecolTournament q N), q >= 4 /\ ~ transitive T /\ longest_coloravoiding T < f q (q-1) N.

classifications.title: Open Question on Circular Drawing Characterisation
classifications.clean_statement: Is there a forbidden-structure characterization theorem of the form: 'every sufficiently/appropriately connected digraph G admits a circular drawing (a drawing in which all directed cycles wind consistently / have a common weight, equivalently a planar/cylindrical layout where all dicycles have the same combinatorial weight) if and only if G contains no substructure of some forbidden type X'? Determine the obstruction class X.
definitions_needed: circular drawing of a digraph (all directed cycles same weight/winding)
classifications.rocq_sketch: (* informal: exists (X : digraphclass), forall G, connected_enough G -> (admits_circular_drawing G <-> forb X G) -- X unknown *)

classifications.title: Conjecture 4.1
classifications.clean_statement: For a connected vertex-transitive digraph D of order n, the 'perimeter gap' is n minus the circumference (length of a longest directed cycle), i.e. the deficit from being Hamiltonian. Conjecture: there exists epsilon > 0 and infinitely many n for which there is a connected vertex-transitive digraph of order n whose perimeter gap is at least epsilon*n (so connected vertex-transitive digraphs can be linearly far from having a Hamiltonian directed cycle).
definitions_needed: vertex-transitive digraph (automorphism group transitive on vertices)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture c41 : exists eps : R, eps > 0 /\ forall N, exists D : vtDigraph, connected D /\ order D >= N /\ (order D - circumference D)%:R >= eps * (order D)%:R.

classifications.title: Conjecture 4.2
classifications.clean_statement: Let c(n) be the minimum circumference (longest cycle length) over all connected vertex-transitive (undirected) graphs on n vertices, and d(n) the minimum circumference (longest directed cycle length) over all connected vertex-transitive digraphs on n vertices. Conjecture: d(n) = Theta(c(n)), i.e. there are constants 0 < a <= b with a*c(n) <= d(n) <= b*c(n) for all n (the directed minimum circumference is of the same order as the undirected one).
classifications.rocq_sketch: Conjecture c42 : exists a b : R, 0 < a /\ forall n, a * (c n) <= (d n)%:R <= b * (c n). (* c n = min undirected VT circumference, d n = min VT digraph circumference *)

classifications.title: Question 4.3
classifications.clean_statement: Does vertex-transitivity guarantee that in every connected vertex-transitive digraph, any two longest directed cycles share at least one common vertex? (Directed analogue of the classical question on intersection of longest cycles, restricted to the vertex-transitive case.)
classifications.rocq_sketch: Question q43 (D : digraph) : vertex_transitiveb D -> connected D -> forall c1 c2 : dicycle D, longest c1 -> longest c2 -> exists v, v \in c1 /\ v \in c2.

classifications.title: Problem 2
classifications.clean_statement: Let c_r denote the maximum number of pairwise openly (internally) vertex-disjoint directed cycles guaranteed in (or the extremal cycle-packing parameter of) every r-regular digraph D (each vertex has outdeg = indeg = r), as defined in the paper. Problem: determine the limit lim_{r -> infinity} c_r / r.
definitions_needed: c_r = extremal disjoint-cycle count for r-regular digraphs
classifications.rocq_sketch: (* determine L : R such that (c_r / r : R) --> L as r --> oo, where c_r = min over r-regular D of max openly-disjoint dicycle packing *)

classifications.title: Informal Question on optimal directed tree-width constant for regular digraphs
classifications.clean_statement: Determine the best-possible (largest) constant epsilon > 0 such that every r-regular digraph D (outdeg = indeg = r at each vertex) has directed tree-width at least epsilon*r. I.e. find sup{ eps | every r-regular D has dtw(D) >= eps*r }.
classifications.rocq_sketch: (* find sup { eps : R | eps > 0 /\ forall (r:nat) (D : regularDigraph r), (eps * r%:R <= (dtw D)%:R) } *)

synthesis.by_status: open ~108; partial 4 (Edge-disjoint Hamilton cycles in tournaments, Jaeger modular orientation, Bermond-Thomassen, Edge-disjoint-Ham); solved 1 (PTAS for FAS in tournaments = Kenyon-Mathieu-Schudy); disproved 1 (Ádám's Conjecture); unknown/open-ended 9 are 'open' but classification-style (no boolean target). NOTE: array truncates inside the final item (Question 5.9) so the tail (~17 more AACL/heroes/misc items toward the stated ~140) is unseen; counts are for the 123 visible records and scale by ~+15 open once the tail is included.
synthesis.by_formalizability: clean ~30 (Seymour-2nd-nbhd, Caccetta-Haggkvist, Two-Color, Woodall, Hoang-Reed-family, Linial-Berge, majority-3-col family, etc.); bnd ~33 (Mader/maderian family, Bermond-Thomassen, twin-width-binding, Gyarfas-Sumner-tournaments, connectivity-maderian); needs-asymptotics ~26 (all flow/Sidorenko/random/Landau growth items, chi-bounded binding-function items, planar 3/5); needs-computation-model ~9 (PTAS-FAS, circular-F-COL complexity, fractional-dichromatic threshold, Berge path-partition min-max, surface-dicolourability, dfs-tree, even-circuit-matroid); hard ~25 (heroes/Forb_ind-dichromatic cluster, infinite-digraph/ends, Burling/orthogonal-decomp, Sidorenko-forcing iff, strong-EH, tournament-local-chromatic, AT-orientation). The 'clean' bucket is where statement-only Rocq ships immediately; 'hard' is dominated by the heroes/dichromatic-binding cluster which is statement-only-cleanly-statable once P2+P6 infra exists.

new_infra_primitives.description: In-degree deg^-(v) = #|N_in v| as a counting function (N_in exists; the numeric in-degree and min/semi-degree predicates do not). Companion: min-outdeg / min-indeg / semidegree delta^0 = min over v of min(deg^+,deg^-). Single cheapest highest-leverage addition; mirror of existing outdeg.
example_conjectures: Long directed cycles in diregular digraphs (Bermond-Germa-Heydemann-Sotteau)
Hamilton cycle in small d-diregular graphs (Jackson)
TT_k subdivision forced by large minimum semi-degree (Conj 3, 1610.00876)
Decomposing an even tournament into directed paths (Alspach-Mason-Pullman)
Arc-disjoint directed cycles in regular digraphs (Alon-McDiarmid-Molloy)

new_infra_primitives.description: Directed girth = length of shortest directed cycle (and the dual 'r-free / no dicycle of length <= r' predicate). Built directly on existing dicycle + path length ell. Underpins the entire Caccetta-Haggkvist cluster.
example_conjectures: Non-edges vs feedback edge sets (Chudnovsky-Seymour-Sullivan, no C_<=3)
r-free digraphs with r>2n/3 one arc from acyclic

new_infra_primitives.description: N^{++}(v) = vertices at directed distance exactly 2 (out-neighbours of out-neighbours, excluding N^+ and v). Tiny add on top of N_out. Carries the single most famous clean open digraph conjecture.

vec-chi(D) = min number of colour classes each inducing an acyclic (dicycle-free) subdigraph; plus 2-colour special case (Two-Color/Neumann-Lara) and fractional relaxation. Already partly proxied by omegabar but the genuine acyclic-partition number is new. The hub of the entire heroes/Aboulker corpus.
example_conjectures: The Two Color Conjecture (Neumann-Lara, planar dichromatic <= 2)
chordal digraphs not chi-bounded construction size (2202.01006)

new_infra_primitives.description: Induced-subdigraph containment predicate ind_contains plus the class Forb_ind(F)/Forb_ind(family) and the 'bounded over the class' quantifier (chi-bounded / vec-chi-finite / heroic). Uses existing induced_digraph + dgiso. Required by every Gyarfas-Sumner / heroes / chi-bounded item.

Oriented-graph + oriented-tree / oriented-forest / oriented-star / transitive-tournament TT_k object family, and the underlying-undirected functor 'is_forest(underlying H)'. Oriented exists; the structured tree/forest/star families and orientation enumeration (Or(P4)) are new.
example_conjectures: Antidirected trees in digraphs (Addario-Berry et al.)
Conj 4.2 heroic iff star-forest or transitive (2009.13319)

new_infra_primitives.description: Arc-strong connectivity lambda / k-arc-strong predicate (also vertex-strong kappa). Needed for the whole arc-disjoint-spanning / branchings / Woodall / connectivity-maderian cluster. Currently absent.
example_conjectures: Decompose k-arc-strong tournament into k spanning strong digraphs
Partitioning a tournament into k-strong subtournaments (Bang-Jensen)

new_infra_primitives.description: Containment of an oriented tree/forest as a subdigraph, and the maderian-threshold wrapper (least m forcing containment under a degree/chi/connectivity parameter). Ties together Mader-type and Sumner-type items. Reuses dgiso/induced.
example_conjectures: Disjoint union of maderian forests is maderian (Conj 7)
Subdivision of TT_k forced by large outdegree (Mader)

new_infra_primitives.description: Subdivision (arcs replaced by internally-disjoint dipaths) containment of a fixed digraph (TT_k, complete digraph). Distinct from subdigraph containment; reuses dipath.
example_conjectures: Subdivision of transitive tournament with large outdegree (Mader)

new_infra_primitives.description: A family of pairwise vertex-disjoint directed cycles + its dual transversal (Erdos-Posa). Also serves arc-disjoint transitive-triangle packing. Built on dicycle + del_vertex.
example_conjectures: Min arc-disjoint transitive triangles in tournaments (Yuster)

new_infra_primitives.description: Out-branching / in-branching / arborescence (spanning out/in tree rooted at a vertex) and arc-disjointness between them; balanced bi-tree. Foundation for the Thomassen branching and temporalization clusters.
example_conjectures: Linear-size balanced bi-tree / forward cover (2304.03567 Problems 1-3, Conj 1)

new_infra_primitives.description: Directed cut (dicut) = arcs entering a set with none leaving; dijoin = arc set meeting every dicut; min-dicut size; arc-disjoint dijoins. Self-contained pair for Woodall.
Partition of V into vertex-disjoint dipaths; k-norm sum_p min(|p|,k); arc-decomposition into dipaths; the min-max duality with k-colourable induced subgraphs. Reuses dipath + induced.
example_conjectures: Decomposing an even tournament into directed paths
Stable set meeting all longest directed paths (Laborde-Payan-Xuong)

new_infra_primitives.description: Feedback arc set: min arc set whose removal makes the digraph acyclic (beta). Distinct from omegabar (back-edges-under-order); FAS is the arc-deletion optimum. Plus the max-over-orientations variant beta(G).
example_conjectures: PTAS for FAS in tournaments (Kenyon-Mathieu-Schudy, SOLVED)
r-free digraphs one arc from acyclic
Suspected directed surplus of random orientation (Remark 1.6)

new_infra_primitives.description: Reverse an arc (u,v)->(v,u); del_arc; and num_dicycles count. Needed for Adam and FAS-via-reversal items. Small.
example_conjectures: Adam's Conjecture (DISPROVED — good negative test case)
r-free digraphs one arc from acyclic

new_infra_primitives.description: Majority colouring: each vertex has at most half (resp. <=(1/k)deg^+) of its out-neighbours in its own colour class; plus list / fractional / choosability variants. Pure function over N_out + outdeg; ships clean.
Oriented colouring / oriented chromatic number = min |C| with a homomorphism to a tournament on C; digraph homomorphism dhom; circular-F-colouring. Reuses Oriented/Tournament/dgiso.
example_conjectures: Oriented chromatic number of planar graphs (Courcelle/Sopena)

new_infra_primitives.description: k-colouring of arcs; rainbow directed triangle; monochromatic directed path / monochromatic reachability. Reuses dipath/dicycle.
example_conjectures: Monochromatic reachability or rainbow triangles (3-coloured tournament)

new_infra_primitives.description: Planarity of (the underlying graph of) a digraph and surface/genus embedding. Heavy structural primitive; needed only by a handful but each is famous.
example_conjectures: Large acyclic induced subdigraph in planar oriented graph (3/5)
Oriented chromatic number of planar graphs

new_infra_primitives.description: Twin-width of a tournament/digraph (contraction-sequence red-degree). Single heavy parameter; only the AACL twin-width-binding item needs it but it is a v1-plan target.
Tree-/path-/spaghetti-decompositions, bag-intersection orthogonality (and minor-closed classes). Heavy width machinery; isolated to the Burling-orthogonal item + minor-closed dichromatic question.

Real-valued quantities: rational fractions (3/5), Landau o/Omega/Theta, exponential counts c^n, graphon densities, real flow values, real thresholds beta<1. Required for every needs-asymptotics item; deepest dependency, ships last.
example_conjectures: Large acyclic induced subdigraph in planar oriented graph (3/5|V|)

new_infra_primitives.description: Group-valued / nowhere-zero r-flow with Kirchhoff conservation, circular flow number F_c, antisymmetric flow, modular orientation, edge-connectivity of undirected graphs, chromatic index. The flow/orientation-of-undirected cluster; borderline out-of-scope for a pure-digraph library.
example_conjectures: Circular flow number of regular class-1 graphs (Steffen)

new_infra_primitives.description: Locally-finite/infinite digraphs, ends, highly-arc-transitive (s-arc-transitive for all s), alternating walks, reachability tiles. Far outside the finite MathComp library; statement-only would need a separate semantic layer. DEFER.

Oriented matroids + even circuits; graphon homomorphism density and (asymmetric/classical) Sidorenko & forcing; oriented Ramsey numbers; strong Erdos-Hajnal; Aharoni rainbow/happy-triples; t(l,k) flash extremal functions. Each is its own theory; mostly hard / needs-asymptotics, low reuse. DEFER as a grab-bag.
example_conjectures: Equivalence of asymmetric and classical Sidorenko
Conjecture 1.5 strong-EH iff forest backedge graph (Pure Pairs X)

marquee_open: Seymour's Second Neighbourhood Conjecture — the single most famous clean open digraph conjecture; statement needs only N^{++} (second-neighbourhood) on top of existing N_out/outdeg; ideal P9 flagship.
Caccetta-Haggkvist Conjecture — top-tier classic; clean statement needs only girth + min-outdeg; anchors the whole short-cycle cluster (bipartite variants, CSS non-edges).
The Two Color Conjecture (Neumann-Lara, planar dichromatic <= 2) — clean and famous; first real consumer of dichromatic + planarity; bridges P2 and P12.
Woodall's Conjecture (min dicut = k => k arc-disjoint dijoins) — clean min-max duality classic (directed Lucchesi-Younger); self-contained dijoin-dicut primitive.
Bermond-Thomassen Conjecture (min-outdeg >= 2k-1 => k vertex-disjoint dicycles) — famous, clean, partial; flagship of P10 vertex-disjoint cycle packing.
Hoang-Reed Conjecture (min-outdeg k => k cycles meeting predecessors in <=1 vertex) — clean, famous, k<=3 known; P10.
Linial-Berge path-partition duality (k-norm min <= max k-colourable) — clean min-max generalising Gallai-Milgram/Greene-Kleitman; flagship of P10 path-partition (needs only path-partition + induced).
Gyarfas-Sumner for Tournaments (Conj 4.3, AACL) — vec-chi-binding iff forest backedge graph; the structural heart of the in-plan tournament-clique folder; cleanly statable once P2+P6 infra exists.
Forb(H) chi-bounded iff underlying H is a forest (Conj 2, 1605.07411) — the marquee chi-bounding dichotomy; in v1 plan; needs Forb_ind + oriented-forest + undirected chi interop (mostly present).
Bang-Jensen-Yeo arc-disjoint strong spanning subdigraphs (SAD) — the in-plan P3 flagship and an active problem folder; clean once arc-connectivity-lambda + strong-spanning-subdigraph land.
synthesis.dedup_notes: Strong overlap, three-way (OPG <-> arXiv <-> 11 folders). FOLDER-BACKED dups already flagged in array: 'Conjecture (Section 9...)' = two_extremal_digraphs (2304.04690); Problem 1.2 / Conj 4.2 / 4.4 / 'minimal heroic families' / Question 1.4 / Question 5.4 all = heroes folder (2009.13319 / heroic_sets_dichromatic); Question 1.1 + 'Delta(1,m,m') hero status' = heroic_sets_hero_forest (2306.04710); 'Open problem on construction size' = chordal_digraph_dichromatic_construction_size (2202.01006); Question 3.5 / Conj 3.12 / 3.13 / 3.16 / 4.3 / 4.11 / 5.3 / 5.6 / Question 5.9 all = tournament_clique_number_omega_cluster + tournament_twinwidth_dichromatic_bounded (2310.04265). OPG<->arXiv & internal-arXiv dups: 'Arc-disjoint strongly connected spanning subdigraphs' (OPG) = Bang-Jensen-Yeo SAD = arc_disjoint folder; 'Decomposing k-arc-strong tournament' is the tournament-restricted SAD variant (near-dup, keep distinct); 'Subdivision of a transitive tournament' (OPG, Mader) overlaps Conj 3 (1610.00876, semi-degree TT_k subdivision) and Mader threshold framing of Conjs 4/7/11/Problem 12 — treat the 1610.00876 maderian items as ONE cluster, 5 records -> ~2 conceptual targets; 'Oriented trees in n-chromatic digraphs' (Burr, OPG) is the chi-version sibling of antidirected-trees (Addario-Berry) and of the maderian-tree items; majority-colouring 1608.03040 = 7 records (Conj2 + Conj9 + OP1,2,3,7,8) collapse to ~3 conceptual targets (general / parametric / variant-relaxations); bipartite Caccetta-Haggkvist Conj 1.5 explicitly subsumes Conj 1.2 (same paper); Conj 4 (1605.07411, oriented stars) is a special case of Conj 2; Sidorenko items 1.4/1.7 + asymmetric-classical (2210.16971) = 1 paper, 3 records -> ~2 targets; tournament-local-chromatic 2305.15585 = Questions 10/Conjs 11/12/13 = 4 records, 1 conceptual family. Net: 123 visible records collapse to ~98 unique conceptual conjectures; ~25 of the visible 123 are duplicate_of-flagged.
synthesis.recommendation: Keep the existing 8 phases as the SPINE and add 6 new phases; the v1 plan's 11 folders already exercise P1-P6 cleanly, so the extension is mostly NEW clusters of OPG/arXiv classics. Concretely: (P9-classic-digraph-core) add indeg + girth + second-neighbourhood + min/semi-degree — three tiny primitives that unlock the highest-fame:lowest-cost ratio in the whole corpus (Seymour-2nd-nbhd, Caccetta-Haggkvist + bipartite variants, long-cycles-in-diregular, Jackson Hamilton, Eulerian/regular degree conditions, CSS non-edges). SHIP THIS FIRST after the current core; ~18-21 clean statements with almost no new structural machinery. (P9b-maderian-containment) oriented-tree/forest/star families + subdigraph & subdivision containment + the maderian-threshold wrapper — collapses the entire 1610.00876 Mader cluster + Burr + antidirected-trees + Mader-TT_k into ~8 statement-only items reusing dgiso/dipath; statement-only is clean (the threshold is just 'exists m'). (P10-packing-duality) arc-disjoint / vertex-disjoint-cycle-packing + branchings/arborescences + dijoin-dicut + path-partition — ships Bermond-Thomassen, Hoang-Reed, Woodall, Linial-Berge, even-tournament-path-decomposition, Erdos-Posa-long-cycles, the bi-tree/forward-cover temporalization items, and the in-plan SAD/connectivity folder; clean-to-bnd, no reals. (P11-colouring-variants) majority-colouring + oriented-chromatic/homomorphism + arc-colouring/monochromatic-reachability — 13+ records, all clean except the complexity ones; ship the majority-colouring family (collapses 7 records) and oriented-chromatic as pure statements, DEFER the circular-F-COL/fractional complexity items to needs-computation-model. (P12-structural-width) planarity + twin-width + tree/path-decomposition — gated, ship Two-Color + planar-3/5 + AACL twin-width-binding (the latter already in plan) and DEFER the orthogonal-decomposition + minor-closed-class items. (P13-flows-undirected) group/nowhere-zero/circular flows + chromatic index + edge-connectivity of undirected graphs — flag as BORDERLINE (orientation-of-undirected, not pure digraph) and ship statement-only only if undirected interop is cheap; 6 records, all needs-asymptotics. (P14-defer-out-of-scope) infinite/arc-transitive digraphs, oriented matroids, Sidorenko/graphon, oriented-Ramsey, strong-EH, Aharoni-rainbow/happy-triples, t(l,k) — ~13 records, low reuse, mark DEFERRED. Cross-cut: P8-reals stays the deepest layer and is a prerequisite for ~26 needs-asymptotics items (all flow density, Landau-growth, graphon, planar-fraction, beta<1 items); do P8 LAST and let everything that only needs the rational-fraction slice (planar 3/5, ceil(n/r)) ship earlier with a thin rational helper rather than full reals. Revised statement-only counts: ~30 ship clean immediately (P9/P9b/P10/P11 core), ~33 ship as bnd 'exists f/m' statements (maderian + binding-function items), ~9 deferred to a computation-model appendix, ~26 gated on P8-reals, ~13 deferred out-of-scope. Sequence: finish core -> P9 (cheapest fame) -> P9b + P10 (containment + packing) -> P2/P6 already-in-plan heroes/dichromatic deepening -> P11 -> P12 -> P8-reals -> P13 -> P14. Highest single ROI action: add the indeg primitive (mirror of outdeg) — it is a dependency of 18 items and currently the library has only N_in (the set) not the count.
